message.content:
# Task: Set up kinsward-site repository

## Context

We're creating a public website + release hosting repo for the game Kinsward (a Dwarf Fortress-inspired colony sim). The game source lives in a private repo. This public repo serves two purposes:
1. Documentation site built with Astro Starlight, deployed to GitHub Pages
2. GitHub Releases hosting for game binaries (cross-repo published from the private repo's CI)

**Owner**: alexeev-dauwalter
**Repo**: kinsward-site (public, already initialized on GitHub)
**Domain**: kinsward.dwltr.life
**Private game repo**: the current repo you're working in

## Step 1: Scaffold the Astro Starlight project

The repo is already cloned. Initialize the project inside it:

```bash
cd kinsward-site  # already cloned
```

Create `package.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "kinsward-site",
  "type": "module",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "astro dev",
    "build": "astro build",
    "preview": "astro preview"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@astrojs/starlight": "^0.34",
    "astro": "^5.7",
    "sharp": "^0.33.0",
    "starlight-kbd": "^0.4"
  }
}
```

Run `npm install`.

## Step 2: Astro config

Create `astro.config.mjs`. Note: `starlight-kbd` is registered as a Starlight plugin:

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import starlight from '@astrojs/starlight';
import starlightKbd from 'starlight-kbd';

export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://kinsward.dwltr.life',
  integrations: [
    starlight({
      title: 'Kinsward',
      description: 'A Dwarf Fortress-inspired colony sim with adventure and overseer modes',
      plugins: [starlightKbd()],
      logo: {
        light: './src/assets/logo-light.svg',
        dark: './src/assets/logo-dark.svg',
        replacesTitle: false,
      },
      social: {
        github: 'https://github.com/alexeev-dauwalter/kinsward-site',
      },
      customCss: ['./src/styles/custom.css'],
      sidebar: [
        {
          label: 'Getting Started',
          items: [
            { label: 'Download', link: '/getting-started/download/' },
            { label: 'Installation', link: '/getting-started/installation/' },
            { label: 'Controls', link: '/getting-started/controls/' },
          ],
        },
        {
          label: 'Gameplay',
          items: [
            { label: 'Adventure Mode', link: '/gameplay/adventure-mode/' },
            { label: 'Overseer Mode', link: '/gameplay/overseer-mode/' },
            { label: 'World Generation', link: '/gameplay/world-generation/' },
            { label: 'Units & Companions', link: '/gameplay/units/' },
            { label: 'Crafting', link: '/gameplay/crafting/' },
          ],
        },
        {
          label: 'kinswardctl',
          items: [
            { label: 'Overview', link: '/gamectl/overview/' },
            { label: 'Commands Reference', link: '/gamectl/commands/' },
            { label: 'Examples', link: '/gamectl/examples/' },
          ],
        },
        {
          label: 'Development',
          items: [
            { label: 'Roadmap', link: '/development/roadmap/' },
            { label: 'Changelog', link: '/development/changelog/' },
            { label: 'Architecture', link: '/development/architecture/' },
          ],
        },
      ],
    }),
  ],
});
```

Create `tsconfig.json`:
```json
{ "extends": "astro/tsconfigs/strict" }
```

## Step 3: Directory structure

```
src/
├── assets/
│   ├── logo-dark.svg
│   ├── logo-light.svg
│   └── screenshots/
├── content/docs/
│   ├── index.mdx                          # splash landing page
│   ├── getting-started/
│   │   ├── download.md
│   │   ├── installation.md
│   │   └── controls.mdx                   # .mdx for <Kbd> component
│   ├── gameplay/
│   │   ├── adventure-mode.md
│   │   ├── overseer-mode.md
│   │   ├── world-generation.md
│   │   ├── units.md
│   │   └── crafting.md
│   ├── gamectl/
│   │   ├── overview.md
│   │   ├── commands.md
│   │   └── examples.md
│   └── development/
│       ├── roadmap.md
│       ├── changelog.md
│       └── architecture.md
├── env.d.ts
└── styles/
    └── custom.css
public/
└── favicon.svg
.github/workflows/
└── deploy.yml
```

## Step 4: Content

### CRITICAL: Source content from the actual codebase

Before writing documentation pages, **read these files from the current game repo** to ensure accuracy:

- `README.md` — overview, gamectl commands, architecture
- `.claude/skills/game-control.md` — full TCP protocol and kinswardctl CLI reference
- `CLAUDE.md` — build commands, architecture, system execution order, world model
- `src/bin/gamectl.rs` — actual CLI subcommands (this is the source of truth for kinswardctl docs)
- `Cargo.toml` — current version number
- `assets/blocks.ron` — block types (if exists)
- `assets/items.ron` — item definitions (if exists)

Use what you find to write accurate docs. Don't invent features that don't exist.

### Landing page (`src/content/docs/index.mdx`)

Use `template: splash` frontmatter. Include:
- Hero section: title "Kinsward", tagline about DF-inspired dual-mode gameplay
- Primary action: "Download Latest" → /getting-started/download/
- Secondary action: "Read the Docs" → /getting-started/installation/
- CardGrid with 4 cards: Adventure Mode, Overseer Mode, Living World, Deep Systems
- Brief "Current State" section mentioning pre-1.0 active development
- "Built with" section: Rust, Bevy 0.18, Avian2D, kinswardctl

### Download page

Link to `https://github.com/alexeev-dauwalter/kinsward-site/releases/latest` for both platforms. Include system requirements (Vulkan GPU, x86_64). Note that releases will appear after CI is configured.

### Controls page (`controls.mdx`)

Use `starlight-kbd`'s `<Kbd>` component for all keyboard shortcuts. Import at top of file:

```mdx
---
title: Controls
---

import { Kbd } from 'starlight-kbd/components'

## Adventure Mode

| Action | Key |
|--------|-----|
| Move North | <Kbd>W</Kbd> |
| Move West | <Kbd>A</Kbd> |
| Move South | <Kbd>S</Kbd> |
| Move East | <Kbd>D</Kbd> |
| Stairs up | <Kbd>Shift+,</Kbd> |
| Stairs down | <Kbd>Shift+.</Kbd> |
| Pick up item | <Kbd>E</Kbd> |
| Drop item | <Kbd>Q</Kbd> |
| Hotbar slots | <Kbd>1</Kbd> through <Kbd>9</Kbd> |
| Switch to Overseer | <Kbd>Tab</Kbd> |
| World Map | <Kbd>M</Kbd> |
| Menu | <Kbd>Escape</Kbd> |
```

Use `<Kbd>` throughout all docs pages where keyboard shortcuts are mentioned (not just the controls page). Any page referencing a key should use `.mdx` extension and import the component.

### kinswardctl docs

These are the most important docs — pull command reference directly from `src/bin/gamectl.rs` (the `Cmd` enum and subcommands) and `.claude/skills/game-control.md`. Include:
- Overview: what it is, why (debugging, automation, AI agents, future multiplayer), how it connects
- Full commands reference: every subcommand with examples
- Practical examples: exploration, building, scripting, JSON mode

**Note**: The binary is currently named `gamectl` in the source. In the docs, refer to it as `kinswardctl` (the public-facing name). Mention that in dev builds it's invoked as `cargo run --bin gamectl --`.

### Gameplay docs

Write based on what actually exists in the codebase. Mark planned/unimplemented features with Starlight's `:::caution[Under Development]` admonition.

### Development section

- **Roadmap**: Phase 0 (current, list what's done vs in progress), Phase 1 (crafting + textures), Phase 2 (overseer UI), Phase 3 (living world). Use checkboxes.
- **Changelog**: Start with current version from Cargo.toml. Summarize what's in the current state.
- **Architecture**: System execution order (GameSet), world model (WorldMap→Layer→Chunk), block system, command layer, climate pipeline, data-driven design (RON files).

## Step 5: Custom theme

Create `src/styles/custom.css` with a dark, game-like feel:
- Dark green accent color scheme (#4c9a6e accent, #1a2332 dark background)
- IBM Plex Sans / IBM Plex Mono fonts (import from Google Fonts)
- Override Starlight CSS variables for both light and dark themes
- Terminal-like code blocks (subtle border)
- Styled download buttons (.action.primary)

## Step 6: Simple logos

Create SVG logos (just a monospace "K" letter):
- `src/assets/logo-dark.svg` — light green (#8fd4a8) on transparent
- `src/assets/logo-light.svg` — dark green (#1a4a30) on transparent

Create `public/favicon.svg` — "K" on dark rounded rect.

## Step 7: GitHub Actions deploy workflow

Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:

```yaml
name: Deploy Site

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - run: echo "kinsward.dwltr.life" > dist/CNAME
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: dist

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
```

## Step 8: Cross-repo release job (in the GAME repo)

In the **current private game repo**, modify `.github/workflows/release.yml` to add a job after `build` and `release`:

```yaml
  publish-to-site:
    needs: [build, release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download all build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Rename artifacts for public release
        run: |
          cd artifacts
          for f in my_game-*; do
            newname=$(echo "$f" | sed 's/my_game/kinsward/')
            mv "$f" "$newname" 2>/dev/null || true
          done
          ls -la

      - name: Create release in kinsward-site
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          repository: alexeev-dauwalter/kinsward-site
          token: ${{ secrets.SITE_REPO_TOKEN }}
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          name: "Kinsward ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          body: |
            ## Kinsward ${{ github.ref_name }}

            ### Downloads
            - **Windows** (x86_64): `kinsward-${{ github.ref_name }}-windows-x86_64.zip`
            - **Linux** (x86_64): `kinsward-${{ github.ref_name }}-linux-x86_64.tar.gz`
          files: |
            artifacts/kinsward-*.tar.gz
            artifacts/kinsward-*.zip
```

## Step 9: Gitignore, env.d.ts, README

- `.gitignore`: node_modules/, dist/, .astro/, .env*, .DS_Store, IDE files
- `src/env.d.ts`: `/// <reference path="../.astro/types.d.ts" />`
- `README.md`: brief description, link to live site, dev commands, note about releases

## Step 10: Verify and push

```bash
npm run build  # must succeed without errors
git add .
git commit -m "feat: initial site with Starlight docs, CI, and custom theme"
git push -u origin main
```

Then tell the user they need to manually:
1. Go to kinsward-site repo → Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions
2. Set custom domain: kinsward.dwltr.life, enable HTTPS
3. Configure DNS: `kinsward.dwltr.life CNAME alexeev-dauwalter.github.io.`
4. Create a Fine-grained PAT (repo scope on kinsward-site: Contents Read+Write)
5. Add secret `SITE_REPO_TOKEN` in the private game repo
6. Wait ~10 min for DNS + SSL

## Important notes

- The game binary is `my_game` in Cargo.toml but should be called `kinsward` in all public-facing docs and release artifacts
- The CLI tool is `gamectl` in source but `kinswardctl` in public docs
- Current game version: read from Cargo.toml (currently 0.14.0)
- Don't invent features — if something isn't in the codebase, mark it as planned
- Use Starlight admonitions: `:::tip`, `:::note`, `:::caution[Under Development]`
- Use `<Kbd>` component from `starlight-kbd` for ALL keyboard shortcut mentions across all docs (not just the controls page). Any page that mentions a key must be `.mdx` and import the component.

1→# Starlight Starter Kit: Basics
     2→
     3→[![Built with Starlight](https://astro.badg.es/v2/built-with-starlight/tiny.svg)](https://starlight.astro.build)
     4→
     5→```
     6→pnpm create astro@latest -- --template starlight
     7→```
     8→
     9→> 🧑‍🚀 **Seasoned astronaut?** Delete this file. Have fun!
    10→
    11→## 🚀 Project Structure
    12→
    13→Inside of your Astro + Starlight project, you'll see the following folders and files:
    14→
    15→```
    16→.
    17→├── public/
    18→├── src/
    19→│   ├── assets/
    20→│   ├── content/
    21→│   │   └── docs/
    22→│   └── content.config.ts
    23→├── astro.config.mjs
    24→├── package.json
    25→└── tsconfig.json
    26→```
    27→
    28→Starlight looks for `.md` or `.mdx` files in the `src/content/docs/` directory. Each file is exposed as a route based on its file name.
    29→
    30→Images can be added to `src/assets/` and embedded in Markdown with a relative link.
    31→
    32→Static assets, like favicons, can be placed in the `public/` directory.
    33→
    34→## 🧞 Commands
    35→
    36→All commands are run from the root of the project, from a terminal:
    37→
    38→| Command                   | Action                                           |
    39→| :------------------------ | :----------------------------------------------- |
    40→| `pnpm install`             | Installs dependencies                            |
    41→| `pnpm dev`             | Starts local dev server at `localhost:4321`      |
    42→| `pnpm build`           | Build your production site to `./dist/`          |
    43→| `pnpm preview`         | Preview your build locally, before deploying     |
    44→| `pnpm astro ...`       | Run CLI commands like `astro add`, `astro check` |
    45→| `pnpm astro -- --help` | Get help using the Astro CLI                     |
    46→
    47→## 👀 Want to learn more?
    48→
    49→Check out [Starlight’s docs](https://starlight.astro.build/), read [the Astro documentation](https://docs.astro.build), or jump into the [Astro Discord server](https://astro.build/chat).

1→# Starlight Starter Kit: Basics
     2→
     3→[![Built with Starlight](https://astro.badg.es/v2/built-with-starlight/tiny.svg)](https://starlight.astro.build)
     4→
     5→```
     6→pnpm create astro@latest -- --template starlight

     9→> 🧑‍🚀 **Seasoned astronaut?** Delete this file. Have fun!
    10→
    11→## 🚀 Project Structure
    12→
    13→Inside of your Astro + Starlight project, you'll see the following folders and files:
    14→
    15→```
    16→.
    17→├── public/
    18→├── src/
    19→│   ├── assets/
    20→│   ├── content/
    21→│   │   └── docs/
    22→│   └── content.config.ts
    23→├── astro.config.mjs
    24→├── package.json
    25→└── tsconfig.json
    26→```
    27→
    28→Starlight looks for `.md` or `.mdx` files in the `src/content/docs/` directory. Each file is exposed as a route based on its file name.
    29→
    30→Images can be added to `src/assets/` and embedded in Markdown with a relative link.
    31→
    32→Static assets, like favicons, can be placed in the `public/` directory.
    33→
    34→## 🧞 Commands
    35→
    36→All commands are run from the root of the project, from a terminal:
    37→
    38→| Command                   | Action                                           |
    39→| :------------------------ | :----------------------------------------------- |
    40→| `pnpm install`             | Installs dependencies                            |
    41→| `pnpm dev`             | Starts local dev server at `localhost:4321`      |
    42→| `pnpm build`           | Build your production site to `./dist/`          |
    43→| `pnpm preview`         | Preview your build locally, before deploying     |
    44→| `pnpm astro ...`       | Run CLI commands like `astro add`, `astro check` |
    45→| `pnpm astro -- --help` | Get help using the Astro CLI                     |
    46→
    47→## 👀 Want to learn more?
    48→
    49→Check out [Starlight’s docs](https://starlight.astro.build/), read [the Astro documentation](https://docs.astro.build), or jump into the [Astro Discord server](https://astro.build/chat).
    50→

1→[package]
     2→name = "kinsward"
     3→version = "0.17.0"
     4→edition = "2021"
     5→
     6→[features]
     7→default = ["debug"]
     8→debug = ["debug-server", "dep:bevy-inspector-egui"]
     9→debug-server = []
    10→dev = ["debug", "bevy/dynamic_linking"]
    11→
    12→[profile.dev]
    13→opt-level = 1
    14→
    15→[profile.dev.package."*"]
    16→opt-level = 3
    17→
    18→[dependencies]
    19→bevy = { version = "0.18.0", default-features = true }
    20→avian2d = "0.5"
    21→bevy-inspector-egui = { version = "0.36.0", optional = true }
    22→serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
    23→serde_json = "1.0"
    24→ron = "0.10"
    25→noise = "0.9"
    26→smallvec = "1.13"
    27→bitflags = "2.6"
    28→clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
    29→image = { version = "0.25", default-features = false, features = ["png"] }
    30→
    31→[lib]
    32→name = "kinsward"
    33→path = "src/lib.rs"
    34→
    35→[[bin]]
    36→name = "kinsward"
    37→path = "src/main.rs"
    38→
    39→[[bin]]
    40→name = "kinswardctl"
    41→path = "src/bin/kinswardctl.rs"
    42→
    43→[[bin]]
    44→name = "worldgen"
    45→path = "src/bin/worldgen.rs"
    46→

1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Build & Run
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→cargo run                              # default (debug features: TCP server + inspector)
     9→cargo run --features dev               # dynamic linking for faster recompilation
    10→cargo build --no-default-features      # release-like build without debug tools
    11→cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- <cmd>        # CLI debug tool (game must be running)
    12→cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- region-map elevation   # export region map layer as PNG
    13→cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42                              # headless worldgen, all layers as PNG
    14→cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --layers elevation --size 1024
    15→cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --map-size 128 --format stats --json  # fast stats
    16→```
    17→
    18→**Feature flags**: `debug` (default) = `debug-server` + `bevy-inspector-egui`; `dev` = `debug` + dynamic linking.
    19→
    20→**Rust toolchain**: nightly required.
    21→
    22→## Testing
    23→
    24→```bash
    25→cargo test                             # all tests
    26→cargo test <test_name>                 # single test
    27→cargo test --lib world::chunk          # tests in a specific module
    28→```
    29→
    30→Tests are inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` within source files, primarily in `src/world/` modules.
    31→
    32→## Architecture
    33→
    34→**Engine**: Bevy 0.18 ECS + avian2d physics. Every subsystem is a Bevy Plugin registered in `main.rs`.
    35→
    36→### System execution order (GameSet)
    37→
    38→```
    39→Input -> Commands -> Movement -> Fov -> Lighting -> Barriers -> Render
    40→```
    41→
    42→Defined in `src/schedule.rs`. Systems must be added to the correct set or ordering breaks.
    43→
    44→### World model
    45→
    46→```
    47→WorldMap (Resource, src/world/map.rs)
    48→  └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>           z-level → Layer (src/world/layer.rs)
    49→     └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>      chunk_pos → Chunk 32×32 (src/world/chunk.rs)
    50→        ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>        dense block grid
    51→        ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    sparse floor covers
    52→        └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  sparse ceiling covers
    53→```
    54→
    55→- `CHUNK_SIZE = 32`, `TILE_SIZE_PX = 32.0`
    56→- `BlockType` is a u16 newtype (not enum) with named constants — extensible without variant explosion
    57→- `BlockRegistry` stores `BlockProps` in a flat `Vec<Option<BlockProps>>` indexed by u16 for O(1) lookup
    58→- Block definitions loaded from `assets/blocks.ron` (RON with `IMPLICIT_SOME` extension), hardcoded fallback if missing
    59→- Item definitions from `assets/items.ron` — both loaded via `std::fs::read_to_string`, not Bevy AssetServer
    60→
    61→### Climate pipeline (`src/generation/climate/`)
    62→
    63→RegionMap 1024×1024 grid, each cell = 16×16 tiles. Pipeline in `src/generation/mod.rs::init_region_map`:
    64→
    65→```
    66→ 1. Plates + Elevation     Voronoi plates, boundary uplift, detail noise
    67→ 2. Terrain Conditioning   thermal erosion, smoothing
    68→ 3. Sea Level              ocean/coast/land BFS, ocean_distance
    69→ 4. Temperature            latitude + lapse rate + ocean proximity
    70→ 5. Pressure               Hadley + thermal + barometric + noise + pressure centers
    71→ 6. Wind                   pressure gradient + Coriolis + terrain deflection + drag
    72→ 7. Rainfall               evaporation + advection + orographic + rain shadow
    73→ 8. Geology                rock types from tectonic context (8 types: RockType enum)
    74→ 9. Depression Fill        priority-flood to remove micro-sinks
    75→10. Drainage               elevation + slope + rock permeability
    76→11. Proto-Rivers           flow accumulation + lake filling
    77→12. Fluvial Erosion        river valleys, modulated by rock hardness
    78→13. Final Rivers           recompute on eroded terrain
    79→14. Biomes                 temperature × rainfall × elevation × drainage (22 BiomeId variants)
    80→15. Fertility              soil fertility from rain + temp + drainage + elevation + rivers
    81→16. Vegetation             plant density from biome + rainfall + temperature + noise
    82→```
    83→
    84→Map view layers: Elevation, Biome, Temperature, Rainfall, Wind, Drainage, Rivers, Geology, Fertility, Vegetation, Plates.
    85→Export: `cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- region-map <layer>` (PNG/ASCII/stats).
    86→
    87→### Coordinate system
    88→
    89→- X = East(+)/West(-), Y = North(+)/South(-), Z = vertical levels (up +)
    90→- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1
    91→- World coords: `tile_x = (translation.x / TILE_SIZE_PX).round() as i32`
    92→
    93→### Key patterns
    94→
    95→**Dirty flags** (`DirtyFlags` resource in `src/world/mod.rs`): tracks what needs recomputation (`active_z_changed`, `render_dirty`, `lighting_dirty`, `fov_dirty`, etc.). Systems check flags and early-return if clean. `DirtyTiles` in render provides per-tile granularity.
    96→
    97→**Command system** (`src/commands/`): All game actions route through `GameCommand` enum (serde-tagged `#[serde(tag = "cmd")]`). Commands pushed to `CommandQueue` resource, processed each frame by `process_commands` / `process_unit_commands`. TCP debug server (port 9999) accepts JSON lines, routes to CommandQueue. Unit commands are partitioned from global commands to avoid ECS query conflicts.
    98→
    99→**Task system** (`src/overseer/task_queue.rs`): Global `TaskQueue` resource stores abstract tasks (Build/Demolish/Pickup) with statuses (Pending/Assigned/Blocked). `TaskAssigner` system auto-assigns Pending tasks to idle units. Failed tasks return to queue as Blocked with retry cooldown. Tasks are created by zone commands (UI) and `TaskCreate` (TCP). TCP commands: QueryTasks, TaskCreate, TaskCancel, TaskCancelZone.
   100→
   101→**State machine** (`src/overseer/mode.rs`): `InputMode` state (`Adventure` | `Overseer`), toggled with B key. Systems use `.run_if(in_state(...))` guards. `OverseerToolMode` is a sub-state for tool selection.
   102→
   103→**UnitZ component**: Tracks z-level independently from `Transform` (which only has 2D x,y). Both Player and Units use this.
   104→
   105→**Surface index** (`src/world/surface_index.rs`): Maps (x,y) columns to walkable z-levels. `resolve_grounding()` in `grounding.rs` determines movement transitions (Walk, StepUp, StepDown, Fall, Blocked).
   106→
   107→### Rendering
   108→
   109→- Tile-based: one Bevy entity per visible tile, managed by `src/render/viewport.rs`
   110→- Sprites from 10px-cell texture atlases (Natural.png, Civilized.png, People.png) scaled 3.2× to match TILE_SIZE_PX
   111→- Final tile color = base_color × depth_factor × light_value × visibility
   112→- Overlay blocks (e.g. LANTERN) render as separate entity on top of base tile
   113→- `SpriteAssets` loaded in `PreStartup` via `TextureAtlasLayout::from_grid()`
   114→
   115→### Module layout
   116→
   117→| Module | Purpose |
   118→|--------|---------|
   119→| `src/world/` | WorldMap, blocks, chunks, layers, surfaces, grounding |
   120→| `src/commands/` | GameCommand enum, executor, TCP debug server, region-map export |
   121→| `src/render/` | Viewport rendering, sprite assets, FPS display |
   122→| `src/fov/` | Shadowcasting FOV, visibility maps |
   123→| `src/lighting/` | Light propagation, ambient/emitter lights |
   124→| `src/generation/` | Procedural world generation, climate pipeline (16 steps) |
   125→| `src/generation/climate/` | Plates, elevation, temperature, pressure, wind, rainfall, geology, drainage, rivers, biomes, fertility, vegetation |
   126→| `src/units/` | Unit components, spawner, possession |
   127→| `src/overseer/` | RTS mode: camera, selection, commands, pathfinding, task queue, UI |
   128→| `src/companions/` | Companion behavior (follow, station, autonomous) |
   129→| `src/ui/` | UI animation system |
   130→
   131→Flat modules: `player.rs` (player entity/movement), `movement_rules.rs` (traversal logic), `physics.rs` (collision layers), `schedule.rs` (system ordering), `items.rs`/`inventory.rs`/`hotbar.rs` (item system), `block_interaction.rs`/`block_item.rs` (block interactions), `camera.rs` (camera control), `elevation_barrier.rs` (elevation barriers), `input.rs` (input handling), `pickup_drop.rs` (pickup/drop), `proximity.rs` (proximity detection), `tooltip.rs` (tooltips), `world_item.rs` (world items).
   132→
   133→## Conventions
   134→
   135→- **Plugins**: `<Name>Plugin`, registered in `main.rs`
   136→- **Components**: PascalCase (`Player`, `UnitZ`, `TileRender`, `PathFollower`)
   137→- **Systems**: snake_case verbs (`spawn_player`, `compute_fov`, `process_commands`)
   138→- **Events**: PascalCase + "Event" suffix (`BlockChangedEvent`, `SpawnUnitEvent`)
   139→- **Feature gates**: `#[cfg(feature = "...")]` inline, not separate files
   140→- **Movement rules**: target tile must NOT have `blocks_movement` at feet level AND must have `supports_standing` below
   141→- All terrain blocks are SOLID (Minecraft-like) — "floor" is a decorative surface cover, not a block type
   142→- Only non-blocking blocks: STAIRS_UP, STAIRS_DOWN, LANTERN, SHALLOW_WATER
   143→- 51 unused warnings exist — known tech debt, don't fix unless touching that code
   144→

1→# Kinsward
     2→
     3→2D voxel-like game with z-levels, built on Bevy 0.18.
     4→
     5→## Build & Run
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→cargo run                              # clean run (no debug UI)
     9→cargo run -- --debug                   # debug mode (admin panel, test items, physics gizmos)
    10→cargo run -- --seed 42                 # fixed world seed
    11→cargo run -- --debug --seed 42         # both
    12→DEBUG=true cargo run                   # debug mode via env var
    13→cargo run --features dev               # dynamic linking (faster recompilation)
    14→cargo build --no-default-features      # without debug-server and inspector
    15→```
    16→
    17→### CLI flags
    18→
    19→| Flag | Description |
    20→|------|-------------|
    21→| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
    22→| `--seed <N>` | World seed (overrides `WORLD_SEED` env var) |
    23→
    24→Env vars: `DEBUG=true` — same as `--debug`. `WORLD_SEED=N` — fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority).
    25→
    26→### Feature flags
    27→
    28→| Feature | Description |
    29→|---------|-------------|
    30→| `debug` (default) | debug-server + bevy-inspector-egui |
    31→| `debug-server` | TCP server on `127.0.0.1:9999` |
    32→| `dev` | debug + dynamic linking (fast iteration) |
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Architecture
    37→
    38→### Coordinate system
    39→
    40→- **X** = East (+) / West (-)
    41→- **Y** = North (+) / South (-)
    42→- **Z** = vertical levels (up +)
    43→- Tile size: 32 px (`TILE_SIZE_PX`)
    44→- Player starts at (0, 0, z=1) on GRASS at z=0
    45→
    46→### World model
    47→
    48→```
    49→WorldMap
    50→  └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>           z-level → Layer
    51→     └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>      chunk pos → Chunk (32x32)
    52→        ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>        dense block storage
    53→        ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    sparse floor covers
    54→        └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  sparse ceiling covers
    55→```
    56→
    57→- **BlockVolume** wraps `BlockType` (u16), occupies z to z+1
    58→- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1
    59→- `block_at_feet` — block at player's z (usually AIR)
    60→- `block_below` — block under player (usually solid terrain)
    61→
    62→### Generation
    63→
    64→Noise-based procedural generation (FBM Perlin):
    65→
    66→| Noise | Octaves | Scale | Determines |
    67→|-------|---------|-------|------------|
    68→| Elevation | 6 | 0.008 | Terrain shape, z-level |
    69→| Moisture | 4 | 0.012 | Biome (grass/mud/sand) |
    70→| Temperature | 1 | 0.006 | Climate (snow/ice) |
    71→| Cave | 3 | 0.05 | Underground caverns |
    72→
    73→Elevation → surface z-level:
    74→
    75→| Elevation | Z | Terrain |
    76→|-----------|---|---------|
    77→| < ~0.12 | -2 | Deep water |
    78→| < 0.35 | -1 | Shallow water |
    79→| < 0.55 | 0 | Plains |
    80→| < 0.70 | 1 | Hills |
    81→| < 0.85 | 2 | Mountains |
    82→| >= 0.85 | 3 | Peaks |
    83→
    84→Water z-level uses a continuous formula: `z = -1 - ((0.35 - elevation) / 0.35 * 1.5) as i32`. Shore (0.35–0.40) generates SAND/ICE blocks at z=0.
    85→
    86→Generation depth: 5 below / 4 above active z-level. Stairs auto-placed at elevation transitions.
    87→
    88→### Surface index & grounding
    89→
    90→Surface index maps `(x, y)` columns to walkable z-levels. Grounding resolution determines movement:
    91→
    92→- Max step up: 1 block
    93→- Fall: unlimited (> 3 blocks = damage, not yet implemented)
    94→- Transitions: Walk, StepUp, StepDown, Fall, Blocked
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Block types
    99→
   100→| ID | Name | ASCII | blocks_movement | supports_standing | Notes |
   101→|----|------|:-----:|:---:|:---:|-------|
   102→| 0 | AIR | `.` | | | Empty space |
   103→| 1 | VOID | ` ` | x | | Impenetrable |
   104→| 2 | HOLE | `o` | | | Vertical shaft, see-through |
   105→| 10 | STONE | `#` | x | x | Solid rock |
   106→| 11 | DIRT | `#` | x | x | Underground fill |
   107→| 20 | GRASS | `#` | | x | Surface terrain |
   108→| 21 | SAND | `#` | | x | Beach / desert |
   109→| 22 | MUD | `#` | | x | Wet terrain |
   110→| 23 | DRY_GRASS | `#` | | x | Arid terrain |
   111→| 24 | SNOW | `#` | | x | Cold terrain |
   112→| 25 | ICE | `#` | x | x | Semi-transparent solid |
   113→| 40 | WALL_STONE | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
   114→| 41 | WALL_WOOD | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
   115→| 42 | GLASS_WALL | `#` | x | | Transparent wall |
   116→| 50 | STAIRS_UP | `^` | | x | Ascending stairs |
   117→| 51 | STAIRS_DOWN | `v` | | x | Descending stairs |
   118→| 60 | DEEP_WATER | `~` | x | | Impassable water |
   119→| 61 | SHALLOW_WATER | `~` | | x | Wadeable |
   120→| 70 | LANTERN | `*` | | | Light source |
   121→
   122→Movement rule: target tile must not have `blocks_movement` at feet level AND must have `supports_standing` below.
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Controls
   127→
   128→### Mode switch
   129→
   130→| Key | Action |
   131→|-----|--------|
   132→| **B** | Toggle Adventure <-> Overseer mode |
   133→
   134→### Adventure mode
   135→
   136→#### Movement
   137→
   138→| Key | Action |
   139→|-----|--------|
   140→| **W/A/S/D** | Move North/West/South/East |
   141→| **Shift** | Sprint |
   142→| **< (Comma)** | Climb stairs up |
   143→| **> (Period)** | Descend stairs down |
   144→
   145→#### Block interaction
   146→
   147→| Input | Action |
   148→|-------|--------|
   149→| **LMB** | Mine block at cursor |
   150→| **RMB** | Place block from hotbar |
   151→| **Ctrl** | Modifier: floor level (z-1) |
   152→| **Alt** | Modifier: ceiling level (z+1) |
   153→| _(no modifier)_ | Wall level (z) |
   154→
   155→Cursor colors: white = wall, blue = floor, orange = ceiling, red = can mine, green = can place.
   156→
   157→#### Hotbar & inventory
   158→
   159→| Input | Action |
   160→|-------|--------|
   161→| **1-0** | Select hotbar slot 1-10 |
   162→| **Scroll wheel** | Cycle hotbar |
   163→| **Tab** | Toggle inventory |
   164→| **Esc** | Close inventory |
   165→| **Q** | Drop item (+Shift = stack, +Ctrl = half) |
   166→| **LMB outside slots** | Drop dragged stack (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |
   167→| **RMB outside slots** | Drop 1 dragged item (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |
   168→
   169→### Overseer mode
   170→
   171→#### Camera
   172→
   173→| Input | Action |
   174→|-------|--------|
   175→| **W/A/S/D** or **Arrows** | Pan camera |
   176→| **MMB drag** | Free pan |
   177→| **Scroll wheel** | Change z-level |
   178→| **PgUp / PgDn** | Jump z-level |
   179→
   180→#### Unit selection
   181→
   182→| Input | Action |
   183→|-------|--------|
   184→| **LMB click** | Select unit (empty = deselect) |
   185→| **LMB drag** | Box select |
   186→| **Shift+LMB** | Toggle / add to selection |
   187→
   188→#### Commands
   189→
   190→| Input | Action |
   191→|-------|--------|
   192→| **RMB** | Issue smart command |
   193→| **Shift+RMB** | Queue command (append) |
   194→| **Esc** | Reset tool to Auto mode |
   195→
   196→Smart action priority: Pickup item > Demolish block > Build (if hotbar has block) > MoveTo.
   197→
   198→Colors: green = move, blue = build, red = demolish, yellow = pickup.
   199→
   200→---
   201→
   202→## TCP debug protocol
   203→
   204→Game listens on `127.0.0.1:9999` (feature `debug-server`, default on). Protocol: JSON lines over TCP — one JSON object + `\n` per command.
   205→
   206→### Response format
   207→
   208→```json
   209→{"id": 1, "ok": true, "data": {...}}
   210→{"id": 2, "ok": false, "data": null, "error": "message"}
   211→```
   212→
   213→### Quick test
   214→
   215→```bash
   216→echo '{"cmd":"Ping"}' | nc -q1 127.0.0.1 9999
   217→```
   218→

   315→
   316→---
   317→
   318→## kinswardctl — CLI for game control
   319→
   320→`kinswardctl` is a command-line tool for controlling the running game via TCP debug server. A wrapper over the protocol above with human-readable output.
   321→
   322→### Setup
   323→
   324→```bash
   325→# Game must be running
   326→cargo run
   327→
   328→# In another terminal:
   329→cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- ping
   330→```
   331→
   332→### Global flags
   333→
   334→| Flag | Description |
   335→|------|-------------|
   336→| `--host <IP>` | Server address (default `127.0.0.1`) |
   337→| `--port <PORT>` | Port (default `9999`) |
   338→| `--json` | Raw JSON output |
   339→
   340→```bash
   341→kinswardctl --json state          # raw JSON
   342→kinswardctl --port 8888 ping      # custom port
   343→```
   344→
   345→### Meta
   346→
   347→```bash
   348→kinswardctl ping                  # test connection
   349→kinswardctl info                  # active_z and basic info
   350→kinswardctl game-info             # seed, layers, chunks, entities
   351→```
   352→
   353→### Player state
   354→
   355→```bash
   356→kinswardctl state
   357→# OK  x=0  y=0  z=1  active_z=1  block_at_feet=AIR  block_below=GRASS  has_support=true
   358→
   359→kinswardctl blocked
   360→# OK  blocked: West, North
   361→
   362→kinswardctl can-move north
   363→# OK  North can_move=false
   364→```
   365→
   366→### Movement
   367→
   368→```bash
   369→kinswardctl move north            # one tile (n/s/e/w)
   370→kinswardctl move-delta 3 0        # offset dx=3, dy=0
   371→kinswardctl stairs up             # climb stairs
   372→kinswardctl stairs down           # descend stairs
   373→kinswardctl teleport 10 5 2       # teleport to (10, 5, z=2)
   374→```
   375→
   376→### World visualization
   377→
   378→```bash
   379→# Top-down layer at z=1, x=-5..5, y=-5..5
   380→kinswardctl layer -z 1 -x -5:5 -y -5:5
   381→
   382→# Side cross-section at y=0, x=-5..5, z=0..3
   383→kinswardctl cross-section -y 0 -x -5:5 -z 0:3
   384→
   385→# 3D volume data (JSON)
   386→kinswardctl volume -x -2:2 -y -2:2 -z 0:2
   387→
   388→# Field of view (ASCII visibility map)
   389→kinswardctl fov --radius 12
   390→
   391→# Nearby items
   392→kinswardctl nearby --radius 10
   393→# OK  3 items within radius 10 of (0,0,1)
   394→#   [4294967302] (1,2) Stone x5  dist=2
   395→#   [4294967310] (-1,0) Apple x1  dist=1
   396→```
   397→
   398→### World modification
   399→
   400→```bash
   401→kinswardctl set-block 5 5 1 STONE
   402→# OK  set (5,5,1) to STONE
   403→
   404→kinswardctl fill -x -3:3 -y -3:3 -z 1 AIR
   405→# OK  filled 49 blocks with AIR
   406→
   407→kinswardctl rebuild-surfaces       # usually auto, manual if needed
   408→```
   409→
   410→### Registry
   411→
   412→```bash
   413→kinswardctl registry blocks        # all block types
   414→kinswardctl registry items         # all items
   415→kinswardctl registry floor-covers  # floor covers
   416→
   417→kinswardctl lookup blocks STONE    # lookup by name or id
   418→kinswardctl lookup items apple     # search items (substring, case-insensitive)
   419→kinswardctl lookup items 102       # search by numeric id
   420→```
   421→
   422→### Unit management
   423→
   424→Units execute queued commands autonomously with A* pathfinding. Works in any input mode.
   425→
   426→```bash
   427→# List units (get entity IDs)
   428→kinswardctl units
   429→# OK  2 unit(s)
   430→#   ID                   Name             Position     Queue  Status
   431→#   4294967305           Worker           (3,2,1)      0      idle
   432→
   433→# Detailed info
   434→kinswardctl unit-info 4294967305
   435→# OK  Unit: Worker (id=4294967305)
   436→#   Position: (3,2,1)
   437→#   Status: idle  Hotbar slot: 0
   438→#   Inventory (2 items):
   439→#     [0] Stone Block x10
   440→#     [1] Apple x3
   441→#   Queue: (empty)
   442→
   443→# Move unit to target
   444→kinswardctl unit-move 4294967305 10 5 1
   445→# OK  queued (queue_len=1)
   446→
   447→# Build block (item_id from registry items)
   448→kinswardctl unit-build 4294967305 10 5 1 102
   449→# OK  queued (queue_len=2)
   450→
   451→# Demolish block
   452→kinswardctl unit-demolish 4294967305 10 6 1
   453→
   454→# Pick up item (target_entity_id from nearby)
   455→kinswardctl unit-pickup 4294967305 4294967310
   456→
   457→# Multi-waypoint route (single TCP connection)
   458→kinswardctl unit-plan 4294967305 5,0,1 5,5,1 0,5,1
   459→#   1. 5,0,1 -> queued (queue_len=1)
   460→#   2. 5,5,1 -> queued (queue_len=2)
   461→#   3. 0,5,1 -> queued (queue_len=3)
   462→# OK  3 waypoints queued for unit 4294967305
   463→
   464→# Clear queue (unit finishes current path segment)
   465→kinswardctl unit-queue-clear 4294967305
   466→
   467→# Full stop (immediate)
   468→kinswardctl unit-stop 4294967305
   469→```
   470→
   471→### Task management
   472→
   473→Global task queue decouples task creation from unit assignment. Tasks are auto-assigned to idle units.
   474→
   475→```bash
   476→kinswardctl tasks                                  # list all tasks
   477→kinswardctl tasks --status pending                 # filter: pending/assigned/blocked
   478→kinswardctl task-add demolish 5 5 1                # create task
   479→kinswardctl task-add build 5 5 1 --item 102        # create build task
   480→kinswardctl task-cancel 42                         # cancel task by ID
   481→kinswardctl task-cancel-zone -x 0:5 -y 0:5 -z 1   # cancel zone tasks
   482→```
   483→
   484→### Raw JSON
   485→
   486→```bash
   487→kinswardctl raw '{"cmd":"QueryState"}'
   488→```
   489→
   490→### Help
   491→
   492→```bash
   493→kinswardctl --help                 # all commands
   494→kinswardctl move --help            # help for specific command
   495→```
   496→
   497→### Tips
   498→
   499→- Start with `state` to get player position and surroundings
   500→- `blocked` is more efficient than 4 `can-move` calls
   501→- `layer` and `cross-section` for quick spatial orientation
   502→- `fill` instead of many `set-block` for bulk operations
   503→- `unit-plan` sends multiple waypoints over a single TCP connection
   504→- `unit-stop` for immediate halt; `unit-queue-clear` lets unit finish current path
   505→- `nearby` returns `entity_id` for items — use with `unit-pickup`
   506→- `--json` flag goes before subcommand: `kinswardctl --json state`
   507→

1→# Game Control via TCP Debug Server
     2→
     3→Control the running game through its TCP debug server on `127.0.0.1:9999`.
     4→
     5→## Connection
     6→
     7→The game must be running with the `debug-server` feature (enabled by default).
     8→Protocol: JSON lines over TCP (each command is a single JSON object terminated by `\n`).
     9→
    10→Connect and send commands using a helper script or inline bash:
    11→
    12→```bash
    13→# One-shot command (send and receive one response):
    14→echo '{"cmd":"Ping"}' | nc -q1 127.0.0.1 9999
    15→
    16→# Multiple commands in a session:
    17→python3 -c "
    18→import socket, json
    19→s = socket.socket(); s.settimeout(5); s.connect(('127.0.0.1', 9999))
    20→def cmd(c):
    21→    s.sendall((json.dumps(c)+'\n').encode())
    22→    buf = b''
    23→    while b'\n' not in buf: buf += s.recv(4096)
    24→    return json.loads(buf)
    25→
    26→# Your commands here:
    27→print(cmd({'cmd': 'QueryState'}))
    28→s.close()
    29→"
    30→```
    31→
    32→## Response Format
    33→
    34→Success: `{"id": N, "ok": true, "data": {...}}`
    35→Error: `{"id": N, "ok": false, "data": null, "error": "message"}`
    36→Parse error: `{"id": -1, "ok": false, "error": "parse error: ..."}`
    37→
    38→## Commands Reference
    39→
    40→### Meta
    41→
    42→| Command | JSON | Response data |
    43→|---------|------|---------------|
    44→| Ping | `{"cmd":"Ping"}` | `{"pong":true}` |
    45→| QueryInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryInfo"}` | `{"active_z": N}` |
    46→| QueryGameInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryGameInfo"}` | `{"world_seed","active_z","loaded_layers","loaded_chunks","entities":[...]}` |
    47→
    48→### Registry
    49→
    50→| Command | JSON | Response data |
    51→|---------|------|---------------|
    52→| QueryRegistry | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistry","kind":"blocks"}` | `{"kind","count","entries":[{"id","name","display_name","blocks_movement","supports_standing",...}]}` |
    53→| QueryRegistry | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistry","kind":"items"}` | `{"kind","count","entries":[{"id","name","group","tag","stack_size"}]}` |
    54→| QueryRegistry | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistry","kind":"floor_covers"}` | `{"kind","count","entries":[{"id","const_name","display_name"}]}` |
    55→| QueryRegistryLookup | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistryLookup","kind":"blocks","query":"STONE"}` | Block properties (by name or numeric id) |
    56→| QueryRegistryLookup | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistryLookup","kind":"items","query":"apple"}` | `{"matches":[...],"count":N}` - search by name/tag/id (case-insensitive substring) |
    57→| QueryRegistryLookup | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistryLookup","kind":"floor_covers","query":"CARPET"}` | Floor cover properties (by name or numeric id) |
    58→
    59→### Queries
    60→
    61→| Command | JSON | Response data |
    62→|---------|------|---------------|
    63→| QueryState | `{"cmd":"QueryState"}` | `{"x","y","z","active_z","block_at_feet","block_below","has_support"}` |
    64→| QueryBlock | `{"cmd":"QueryBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` | `{"x","y","z","block_type","blocks_movement","supports_standing"}` |
    65→| QuerySurfaces | `{"cmd":"QuerySurfaces","x":N,"y":N}` | `{"x","y","surfaces":[z1,z2,...]}` - walkable z-levels |
    66→| QueryCanMove | `{"cmd":"QueryCanMove","dir":"North"}` | `{"dir","can_move":bool}` |
    67→| QueryBlocked | `{"cmd":"QueryBlocked"}` | `{"x","y","z","blocked":["North",...]}` |
    68→| QueryLayer | `{"cmd":"QueryLayer","z":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N}` | `{"ascii":"...","z":N}` - XY top-down view |
    69→| QueryCrossSection | `{"cmd":"QueryCrossSection","y":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` | `{"ascii":"...","y":N}` - XZ side view |
    70→| QueryVolume | `{"cmd":"QueryVolume","x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` | `{"layers":[...],"surfaces":{...},"player":{...}}` - 3D block data (max 4000 blocks) |
    71→| QueryNearby | `{"cmd":"QueryNearby","radius":N}` | `{"x","y","z","radius","items":[{"entity_id":N,"x":N,"y":N,"name":"...","id":N,"count":N,"distance":N},...]}` |
    72→| QueryFOV | `{"cmd":"QueryFOV","radius":N}` | `{"ascii":"...","visible":N,"explored":N}` - visibility map around player |
    73→
    74→### Movement
    75→
    76→| Command | JSON | Response data |
    77→|---------|------|---------------|
    78→| MoveDir | `{"cmd":"MoveDir","dir":"North"}` | `{"x","y","z","transition"}` |
    79→| MoveDelta | `{"cmd":"MoveDelta","dx":N,"dy":N}` | `{"x","y","z","transition"}` |
    80→| StairsUp | `{"cmd":"StairsUp"}` | `{"x","y","z","transition":"StairsUp"}` |
    81→| StairsDown | `{"cmd":"StairsDown"}` | `{"x","y","z","transition":"StairsDown"}` |
    82→| Teleport | `{"cmd":"Teleport","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` | `{"x","y","z","transition":"Teleport"}` |
    83→
    84→Directions: `"North"` (+Y), `"South"` (-Y), `"East"` (+X), `"West"` (-X).
    85→Movement validates physics (blocking, support). Teleport bypasses validation.
    86→
    87→### World Modification
    88→
    89→| Command | JSON | Response data |
    90→|---------|------|---------------|
    91→| SetBlock | `{"cmd":"SetBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"block_type":"STONE"}` | `{"x","y","z","block_type"}` |
    92→| FillArea | `{"cmd":"FillArea","x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z":N,"block_type":"FLOOR"}` | `{"filled":N,"block_type","z"}` |
    93→| MineBlock | `{"cmd":"MineBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` | `{"x","y","z","block_type","item_id"}` |
    94→| PlaceBlock | `{"cmd":"PlaceBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"item_id":102}` | `{"x","y","z","block_type","item_id"}` |
    95→| RebuildSurfaces | `{"cmd":"RebuildSurfaces"}` | `{"status":"..."}` |
    96→
    97→After modifying blocks, surfaces are rebuilt automatically per-column.
    98→
    99→### Inventory
   100→
   101→| Command | JSON | Response data |
   102→|---------|------|---------------|
   103→| PickupNearest | `{"cmd":"PickupNearest"}` | `{"item_id","name","count"}` |
   104→| DropFromSlot | `{"cmd":"DropFromSlot","slot":0,"count":1}` | `{"item_id","name","count","x","y","z"}` |
   105→| SelectHotbar | `{"cmd":"SelectHotbar","slot":2}` | `{"selected":2}` |
   106→Use `RebuildSurfaces` only if you suspect surface index is stale.
   107→
   108→### Unit Management
   109→
   110→Unit commands work in **any input mode** (Adventure or Overseer). Units execute their command queue autonomously with A* pathfinding. Path and queue are visualized with gizmos in any mode.
   111→
   112→| Command | JSON | Response data |
   113→|---------|------|---------------|
   114→| QueryUnits | `{"cmd":"QueryUnits"}` | `{"units":[{"entity_id":N,"name":"...","x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"queue_len":N,"is_moving":bool}]}` |
   115→| QueryUnitInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryUnitInfo","entity_id":N}` | `{"entity_id","name","x","y","z","inventory":[...],"hotbar_selected","queue":[...],"is_moving"}` |
   116→| UnitMoveTo | `{"cmd":"UnitMoveTo","entity_id":N,"x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` | `{"entity_id","queue_len"}` |
   117→| UnitBuild | `{"cmd":"UnitBuild","entity_id":N,"x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"item_id":N}` | `{"entity_id","queue_len"}` |
   118→| UnitDemolish | `{"cmd":"UnitDemolish","entity_id":N,"x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` | `{"entity_id","queue_len"}` |
   119→| UnitPickup | `{"cmd":"UnitPickup","entity_id":N,"target_entity_id":N}` | `{"entity_id","queue_len"}` |
   120→| UnitQueueClear | `{"cmd":"UnitQueueClear","entity_id":N}` | `{"entity_id","cleared":N}` |
   121→| UnitStop | `{"cmd":"UnitStop","entity_id":N}` | `{"entity_id","cleared":N,"stopped":true}` |
   122→| UnitCancelTask | `{"cmd":"UnitCancelTask","entity_id":N,"tile":[x,y,z],"index":N}` | `{"entity_id","cancelled":N}` |
   123→| UnitFollow | `{"cmd":"UnitFollow","follower_id":N,"target_id":N}` | `{"follower_id","behavior":"Follow"}` |
   124→
   125→Entity IDs are obtained from `QueryUnits`. Item entity IDs for pickup are obtained from `QueryNearby`. All IDs are stable within a game session but change between restarts.
   126→
   127→**Queue behavior:** MoveTo, Build, Demolish, Pickup are appended to the unit's command queue. The unit pathfinds and executes them sequentially. Build/Demolish auto-pathfind to an adjacent tile if not in reach.
   128→
   129→**Stopping:** `UnitQueueClear` removes pending commands but the unit finishes its current path segment. `UnitStop` immediately halts all movement and clears the queue.
   130→
   131→**Cancelling:** `UnitCancelTask` removes specific tasks from a unit's queue — either by queue index (`"index": N`) or by tile position (`"tile": [x,y,z]`). Both fields are optional; if both are omitted, no tasks are removed.
   132→
   133→**Following:** `UnitFollow` sets a unit's companion behavior to Follow. The follower must have a Companion component.
   134→
   135→Inventory in `QueryUnitInfo` returns only non-empty slots: `[{"slot":N,"item_id":N,"name":"...","count":N}]`.
   136→Queue items: `[{"type":"MoveTo","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}, {"type":"Build","x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"item_id":N}, {"type":"Demolish","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}, {"type":"Pickup","entity_id":N}]`.
   137→
   138→### Zone Commands
   139→
   140→Distribute tasks across multiple units for bulk demolish/build operations. Tasks are distributed greedily (nearest-first) with a max of 5 tasks per unit per batch.
   141→
   142→| Command | JSON | Response data |
   143→|---------|------|---------------|
   144→| ZoneDemolish | `{"cmd":"ZoneDemolish","entity_ids":[N,...],"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z":N,"append":false}` | `{"tiles":N,"queued":N}` |
   145→| ZoneBuild | `{"cmd":"ZoneBuild","entity_ids":[N,...],"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z":N,"item_id":N,"append":false}` | `{"tiles":N,"queued":N}` |
   146→| ZoneCancel | `{"cmd":"ZoneCancel","entity_ids":[N,...],"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z":N}` | `{"cancelled":N}` |
   147→
   148→`append: false` (default) clears each unit's queue before assigning new tasks. `append: true` adds to the existing queue.
   149→`ZoneCancel` removes any Build/Demolish tasks in the specified zone from the listed units' queues.
   150→
   151→### Spawning
   152→
   153→| Command | JSON | Response data |
   154→|---------|------|---------------|
   155→| SpawnUnit | `{"cmd":"SpawnUnit","kind":"worker","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` | `{"kind","x","y","z","status":"queued"}` |
   156→| SpawnUnit (full) | `{"cmd":"SpawnUnit","kind":"worker","x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"name":"Alice","faction":"colony","controlled":true}` | `{"kind","x","y","z","status":"queued"}` |
   157→| PossessUnit | `{"cmd":"PossessUnit","entity_id":N}` | `{"entity_id","status":"queued"}` |
   158→| PossessUnit (release) | `{"cmd":"PossessUnit"}` | `{"entity_id":null,"status":"queued"}` |
   159→
   160→Unit kinds: `worker`, `miner`, `builder`, `scout`, `goblin`, `deer`, etc.
   161→Factions: `colony`, `hostile`, `neutral`, `wild`.
   162→Omit `entity_id` in `PossessUnit` to return control to the original player.
   163→
   164→### Companions
   165→
   166→Companions are colony units that follow the player. They have independent behavior modes.
   167→
   168→| Command | JSON | Response data |
   169→|---------|------|---------------|
   170→| CompanionList | `{"cmd":"CompanionList"}` | `{"companions":[{"entity_id":N,"name":"...","behavior":"Follow","follow_distance":N,"z":N},...]}` |
   171→| CompanionSetBehavior | `{"cmd":"CompanionSetBehavior","entity_id":N,"behavior":"follow"}` | `{"entity_id":N,"behavior":"follow"}` |
   172→| CompanionCallAll | `{"cmd":"CompanionCallAll"}` | `{"called":N}` |
   173→| CompanionDismissAll | `{"cmd":"CompanionDismissAll"}` | `{"dismissed":N}` |
   174→
   175→Behavior values: `follow` (trail the player), `station` (stay in place), `auto` (act autonomously), `hold` (hold current position).
   176→
   177→### Region Map
   178→
   179→Export region map layers as PNG, ASCII, or statistics for generation diagnostics.
   180→
   181→| Command | JSON | Response data |
   182→|---------|------|---------------|
   183→| QueryRegionMap (PNG) | `{"cmd":"QueryRegionMap","layer":"elevation","format":"png"}` | `{"format":"png","path":"/tmp/region_map_elevation.png","size":[512,512],"bounds":{...}}` |
   184→| QueryRegionMap (ASCII) | `{"cmd":"QueryRegionMap","layer":"rivers","format":"ascii","width":120}` | `{"format":"ascii","ascii":"...","legend":"...","size":[120,60],"bounds":{...}}` |
   185→| QueryRegionMap (stats) | `{"cmd":"QueryRegionMap","layer":"elevation","format":"stats"}` | `{"format":"stats","count","min","max","mean","std_dev","p10-p90","histogram",[...],"water_pct","land_pct"}` |
   186→
   187→**Layers:** `elevation`, `temperature`, `rainfall`, `wind`, `drainage`, `rivers`, `geology`, `fertility`, `vegetation`, `plates`
   188→
   189→**Optional parameters:**
   190→- `path` — PNG output path (default: `/tmp/region_map_{layer}.png`)
   191→- `x_min`, `x_max`, `y_min`, `y_max` — bounds in region-cell coordinates (0..1023, default: full map)
   192→- `size` — output image size in pixels (default: 512, max: 4096)
   193→- `width` — ASCII terminal width (default: 100)
   194→
   195→## Block Types
   196→
   197→| Name | ASCII | Properties |
   198→|------|-------|------------|
   199→| AIR | `.` | empty space |
   200→| VOID | ` ` | impenetrable void |
   201→| HOLE | `o` | vertical shaft |
   202→| STONE | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing |
   203→| DIRT | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing |
   204→| GRASS | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing |
   205→| SAND | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing |
   206→| MUD | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing |
   207→| DRY_GRASS | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing |
   208→| SNOW | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing |
   209→| ICE | `#` | solid, blocks movement, supports standing, semi-transparent |
   210→| WALL_STONE | `#` | wall, blocks movement, does NOT support standing |
   211→| WALL_WOOD | `#` | wall, blocks movement, does NOT support standing |
   212→| GLASS_WALL | `#` | wall, blocks movement, does NOT support standing, transparent |
   213→| STAIRS_UP | `^` | ascending stairs |
   214→| STAIRS_DOWN | `v` | descending stairs |
   215→| DEEP_WATER | `~` | impassable water |
   216→| SHALLOW_WATER | `~` | wadeable water |
   217→| LANTERN | `*` | light source |
   218→
   219→## World Model
   220→
   221→- Coordinate system: X = East/West, Y = North/South, Z = vertical levels
   222→- A block at (x, y, z) occupies the volume from z to z+1
   223→- A player at z=1 stands on top of the block at z=0
   224→- `block_at_feet` is the block at the player's z-level (usually AIR)
   225→- `block_below` is what the player stands on (usually GRASS/FLOOR/STONE)
   226→- Movement checks: target tile must not have `blocks_movement` at feet-level and must have `supports_standing` below
   227→
   228→## kinswardctl CLI
   229→
   230→The `kinswardctl` binary provides a command-line interface to all commands above:
   231→
   232→```bash
   233→kinswardctl ping                      # test connection
   234→kinswardctl state                     # player position and state
   235→kinswardctl blocked                   # show blocked directions
   236→kinswardctl nearby --radius 10        # items nearby (with entity IDs for pickup)
   237→kinswardctl fov --radius 5            # visibility map
   238→kinswardctl registry blocks           # list all block types
   239→kinswardctl registry items            # list all items
   240→kinswardctl registry floor-covers     # list all floor covers
   241→kinswardctl lookup blocks STONE       # lookup block by name or id
   242→kinswardctl lookup items apple        # search items by name/tag/id
   243→kinswardctl game-info                 # extended game info
   244→
   245→# Movement (immediate, tile-by-tile)
   246→kinswardctl move north                # move one tile (n/s/e/w)
   247→kinswardctl teleport 5 5 1            # teleport to coordinates
   248→
   249→# Inventory
   250→kinswardctl pickup-nearest                   # pick up nearest item
   251→kinswardctl drop-from-slot 0 5               # drop 5 items from slot 0
   252→kinswardctl select-hotbar 2                  # select hotbar slot 2
   253→
   254→# World modification
   255→kinswardctl mine-block 5 5 1              # mine block at (5,5,1) -> adds item to inventory
   256→kinswardctl place-block 5 5 1 102         # place item 102 at (5,5,1) -> deducts from inventory
   257→kinswardctl set-block 5 5 1 STONE     # place block
   258→kinswardctl fill -x -3:3 -y -3:3 -z 1 AIR  # fill area
   259→
   260→# Unit management
   261→kinswardctl units                          # list all units (get entity IDs)
   262→kinswardctl unit-info <ID>                 # position, inventory, queue
   263→kinswardctl unit-move <ID> 5 5 1           # queue move to (5,5,1)
   264→kinswardctl unit-build <ID> 5 5 1 102      # queue build (item_id=102)
   265→kinswardctl unit-demolish <ID> 5 5 1       # queue demolish
   266→kinswardctl unit-pickup <ID> <ITEM_EID>    # queue pickup (item entity ID from nearby)
   267→kinswardctl unit-plan <ID> 5,0,1 5,5,1 0,5,1  # queue multiple waypoints at once
   268→kinswardctl unit-queue-clear <ID>          # clear pending commands
   269→kinswardctl unit-stop <ID>                 # immediate full stop
   270→
   271→# Zone operations (bulk task distribution across multiple units)
   272→kinswardctl zone-demolish --units 1234,5678 -x -5:5 -y -5:5 -z 1  # demolish zone
   273→kinswardctl zone-build --units 1234,5678 -x 0:4 -y 0:4 -z 1 --item 102  # build zone
   274→kinswardctl zone-demolish --units 1234 -x 0:9 -y 0:9 -z 1 --append  # append to queue
   275→kinswardctl zone-cancel --units 1234,5678 -x 0:4 -y 0:4 -z 1       # cancel tasks in zone
   276→
   277→# Task cancellation & following
   278→kinswardctl unit-cancel-task <ID> --tile 5,5,1         # cancel tasks at tile (5,5,1)
   279→kinswardctl unit-cancel-task <ID> --index 0            # cancel task at queue index 0
   280→kinswardctl unit-follow <FOLLOWER_ID> <TARGET_ID>      # make unit follow another
   281→
   282→# Spawning
   283→kinswardctl spawn worker 0 0 1                           # spawn worker at (0,0,1)
   284→kinswardctl spawn goblin 5 5 1 --faction hostile         # spawn hostile goblin
   285→kinswardctl spawn worker 0 0 1 --name Alice --controlled # spawn player-controlled unit
   286→kinswardctl possess <ID>                                 # possess unit (switch control)
   287→kinswardctl possess                                      # release possession (return to player)
   288→
   289→# Region map (generation diagnostics)
   290→kinswardctl region-map elevation                     # full elevation map as PNG
   291→kinswardctl region-map elevation --size 1024         # larger resolution
   292→kinswardctl region-map rivers --format ascii --width 120  # ASCII rivers
   293→kinswardctl region-map elevation --format stats      # distribution statistics
   294→kinswardctl region-map temperature --x 200:600 --y 200:600  # subregion
   295→
   296→# Companions
   297→kinswardctl companion-list                    # list all companions with behavior
   298→kinswardctl companion-behavior <ID> follow    # set behavior: follow/station/auto/hold
   299→kinswardctl companion-call-all                # set all companions to Follow
   300→kinswardctl companion-dismiss-all             # set all companions to Station
   301→```
   302→
   303→Use `--json` flag (before subcommand) for raw JSON output: `kinswardctl --json state`
   304→
   305→## Tips
   306→
   307→- Use `QueryState` first to get player position and orientation
   308→- Use `QueryBlocked` to see all blocked directions at once (more efficient than 4x QueryCanMove)
   309→- Use `QueryLayer` and `QueryCrossSection` to visualize the world around the player
   310→- For large-scale building, use `FillArea` instead of multiple `SetBlock` calls
   311→- Use `QueryRegistry` to discover available block types, items, and floor covers
   312→- Use `QueryGameInfo` for world seed, loaded layers/chunks count, and entity positions
   313→- Use `unit-plan` to queue multiple waypoints in a single TCP connection (more efficient)
   314→- Use `unit-stop` to cancel everything immediately; `unit-queue-clear` lets current path finish
   315→- `QueryNearby` returns `entity_id` for each item — use with `unit-pickup`
   316→- Movement returns `"blocked"` error if the target tile is impassable
   317→- Use `region-map` with `--format png` to export map layers as PNG files (can be read with Read tool for visual analysis)
   318→- Use `region-map` with `--format stats` for numerical analysis of map layer distributions
   319→- All 11 map layers can be exported: elevation, temperature, rainfall, wind, drainage, rivers, geology, fertility, vegetation, plates, biome
   320→
   321→## Headless World Generation (worldgen)
   322→
   323→For environments without GPU/display (CI, remote), use `worldgen`:
   324→
   325→    cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --json
   326→
   327→Runs the full 17-step worldgen pipeline and exports region map layers.
   328→No running game instance required. No GPU required.
   329→
   330→### Workflow for analyzing worldgen:
   331→1. Generate: `cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --out /tmp/maps/ --json`
   332→2. View PNGs with the Read tool
   333→3. Check stats: `cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --format stats --json`
   334→4. Modify generation code
   335→5. Regenerate and compare
   336→
   337→### Options:
   338→- `--seed N` — world seed (default: 42)
   339→- `--layers L1,L2,...` — comma-separated layers or "all" (default: all)
   340→  Valid: elevation, temperature, rainfall, wind, drainage, rivers, geology, fertility, vegetation, plates, biome
   341→- `--format png|ascii|stats|all` — output format (default: png)
   342→- `--size N` — PNG resolution in pixels (default: 512, max: 4096)
   343→- `--width N` — ASCII terminal width (default: 100)
   344→- `--map-size N` — region cells per side (default: 1024; use 128 for fast tests)
   345→- `--bounds x_min,y_min,x_max,y_max` — subregion (default: full map)
   346→- `--out DIR` — output directory (default: current)
   347→- `--json` — machine-readable JSON on stdout
   348→

   451→// ── Range parsing ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   452→
   453→fn parse_range(s: &str) -> Result<(i32, i32), String> {
   454→    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.splitn(2, ':').collect();
   455→    if parts.len() != 2 {
   456→        return Err(format!("expected MIN:MAX format, got '{}'", s));
   457→    }
   458→    let min: i32 = parts[0]
   459→        .parse()
   460→        .map_err(|_| format!("invalid number: '{}'", parts[0]))?;
   461→    let max: i32 = parts[1]
   462→        .parse()
   463→        .map_err(|_| format!("invalid number: '{}'", parts[1]))?;
   464→    Ok((min, max))
   465→}
   466→
   467→fn parse_direction(s: &str) -> Result<&'static str, String> {
   468→    match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
   469→        "north" | "n" => Ok("North"),
   470→        "south" | "s" => Ok("South"),
   471→        "east" | "e" => Ok("East"),
   472→        "west" | "w" => Ok("West"),
   473→        _ => Err(format!(
   474→            "unknown direction '{}', expected: north/south/east/west (or n/s/e/w)",
   475→            s
   476→        )),
   477→    }
   478→}
   479→

1→//! Global system ordering sets.
     2→//!
     3→//! Each set represents a logical phase of the game loop.
     4→//! Replaces fragile `.after(concrete_function)` ordering with centralized sets.
     5→
     6→use bevy::prelude::*;
     7→
     8→use crate::game_phase::GamePhase;
     9→
    10→#[derive(SystemSet, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
    11→pub enum GameSet {
    12→    /// Player input processing (WASD, mouse, keyboard)
    13→    Input,
    14→    /// Command processing (TCP, overseer commands)
    15→    Commands,
    16→    /// Player/unit movement, stair transitions, terrain sync
    17→    Movement,
    18→    /// Field of view computation
    19→    Fov,
    20→    /// Lighting computation (after FOV)
    21→    Lighting,
    22→    /// Elevation barrier updates (after FOV + movement)
    23→    Barriers,
    24→    /// Tile rendering updates (after lighting + FOV)
    25→    Render,
    26→}
    27→
    28→pub struct SchedulePlugin;
    29→
    30→impl Plugin for SchedulePlugin {
    31→    fn build(&self, app: &mut App) {
    32→        let playing = in_state(GamePhase::Playing);
    33→        app.configure_sets(
    34→            Update,
    35→            (
    36→                GameSet::Input.run_if(playing.clone()),
    37→                GameSet::Commands.after(GameSet::Input).run_if(playing.clone()),
    38→                GameSet::Movement
    39→                    .after(GameSet::Input)
    40→                    .after(GameSet::Commands)
    41→                    .run_if(playing.clone()),
    42→                GameSet::Fov.after(GameSet::Movement).run_if(playing.clone()),
    43→                GameSet::Lighting.after(GameSet::Fov).run_if(playing.clone()),
    44→                GameSet::Barriers.after(GameSet::Fov).run_if(playing.clone()),
    45→                GameSet::Render
    46→                    .after(GameSet::Lighting)
    47→                    .after(GameSet::Barriers)
    48→                    .run_if(playing),
    49→            ),
    50→        );
    51→    }
    52→}
    53→

1→name: Release Build
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    tags:
     6→      - "v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+*"
     7→
     8→env:
     9→  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
    10→  BINARY_NAME: kinsward
    11→  VERSION: ${{ github.ref_name }}
    12→
    13→permissions:
    14→  contents: write
    15→
    16→jobs:
    17→  build:
    18→    strategy:
    19→      matrix:
    20→        include:
    21→          - os: ubuntu-latest
    22→            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
    23→            suffix: linux-x86_64
    24→            binary_ext: ""
    25→          - os: windows-latest
    26→            target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
    27→            suffix: windows-x86_64
    28→            binary_ext: ".exe"
    29→
    30→    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    31→
    32→    steps:
    33→      - name: Checkout
    34→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    35→
    36→      - name: Install Rust toolchain
    37→        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
    38→
    39→      - name: Cache cargo registry & build
    40→        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
    41→        with:
    42→          key: ${{ matrix.target }}
    43→
    44→      # Linux: install Bevy system dependencies
    45→      - name: Install Linux dependencies
    46→        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
    47→        run: |
    48→          sudo apt-get update
    49→          sudo apt-get install -y \
    50→            pkg-config \
    51→            libx11-dev \
    52→            libasound2-dev \
    53→            libudev-dev \
    54→            libxkbcommon-x11-dev \
    55→            libwayland-dev \
    56→            libxkbcommon-dev \
    57→            libvulkan-dev
    58→
    59→      - name: Build release
    60→        run: cargo build --release --no-default-features
    61→
    62→      - name: Prepare artifact (Linux)
    63→        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
    64→        run: |
    65→          mkdir -p artifact
    66→          cp target/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }} artifact/
    67→          cp -r assets artifact/ 2>/dev/null || true
    68→          cd artifact
    69→          tar czf ../${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.tar.gz *
    70→
    71→      - name: Prepare artifact (Windows)
    72→        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
    73→        shell: pwsh
    74→        run: |
    75→          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path artifact
    76→          Copy-Item "target/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}.exe" artifact/
    77→          if (Test-Path "assets") { Copy-Item -Recurse "assets" artifact/ }
    78→          Compress-Archive -Path artifact/* -DestinationPath "${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.zip"
    79→
    80→      - name: Upload artifact
    81→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
    82→        with:
    83→          name: ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}
    84→          path: |
    85→            ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.tar.gz
    86→            ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.zip
    87→          if-no-files-found: ignore
    88→
    89→  release:
    90→    needs: build
    91→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    92→
    93→    steps:
    94→      - name: Download all artifacts
    95→        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
    96→        with:
    97→          path: artifacts
    98→          merge-multiple: true
    99→
   100→      - name: Create GitHub Release
   101→        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
   102→        with:
   103→          generate_release_notes: true
   104→          files: |
   105→            artifacts/*.tar.gz
   106→            artifacts/*.zip

1→# Kinsward
     2→
     3→2D voxel-like game with z-levels, built on Bevy 0.18.
     4→
     5→## Build & Run
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→cargo run                              # clean run (no debug UI)
     9→cargo run -- --debug                   # debug mode (admin panel, test items, physics gizmos)
    10→cargo run -- --seed 42                 # fixed world seed
    11→cargo run -- --debug --seed 42         # both
    12→DEBUG=true cargo run                   # debug mode via env var
    13→cargo run --features dev               # dynamic linking (faster recompilation)
    14→cargo build --no-default-features      # without debug-server and inspector
    15→```
    16→
    17→### CLI flags
    18→
    19→| Flag | Description |
    20→|------|-------------|
    21→| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
    22→| `--seed <N>` | World seed (overrides `WORLD_SEED` env var) |
    23→
    24→Env vars: `DEBUG=true` — same as `--debug`. `WORLD_SEED=N` — fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority).
    25→
    26→### Feature flags
    27→
    28→| Feature | Description |
    29→|---------|-------------|
    30→| `debug` (default) | debug-server + bevy-inspector-egui |
    31→| `debug-server` | TCP server on `127.0.0.1:9999` |
    32→| `dev` | debug + dynamic linking (fast iteration) |
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Architecture
    37→
    38→### Coordinate system
    39→
    40→- **X** = East (+) / West (-)
    41→- **Y** = North (+) / South (-)
    42→- **Z** = vertical levels (up +)
    43→- Tile size: 32 px (`TILE_SIZE_PX`)
    44→- Player starts at (0, 0, z=1) on GRASS at z=0
    45→
    46→### World model
    47→
    48→```
    49→WorldMap
    50→  └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>           z-level → Layer
    51→     └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>      chunk pos → Chunk (32x32)
    52→        ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>        dense block storage
    53→        ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    sparse floor covers
    54→        └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  sparse ceiling covers
    55→```
    56→
    57→- **BlockVolume** wraps `BlockType` (u16), occupies z to z+1
    58→- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1
    59→- `block_at_feet` — block at player's z (usually AIR)
    60→- `block_below` — block under player (usually solid terrain)
    61→
    62→### Generation
    63→
    64→Noise-based procedural generation (FBM Perlin):
    65→
    66→| Noise | Octaves | Scale | Determines |
    67→|-------|---------|-------|------------|
    68→| Elevation | 6 | 0.008 | Terrain shape, z-level |
    69→| Moisture | 4 | 0.012 | Biome (grass/mud/sand) |
    70→| Temperature | 1 | 0.006 | Climate (snow/ice) |
    71→| Cave | 3 | 0.05 | Underground caverns |
    72→
    73→Elevation → surface z-level:
    74→
    75→| Elevation | Z | Terrain |
    76→|-----------|---|---------|
    77→| < ~0.12 | -2 | Deep water |
    78→| < 0.35 | -1 | Shallow water |
    79→| < 0.55 | 0 | Plains |
    80→| < 0.70 | 1 | Hills |
    81→| < 0.85 | 2 | Mountains |
    82→| >= 0.85 | 3 | Peaks |
    83→
    84→Water z-level uses a continuous formula: `z = -1 - ((0.35 - elevation) / 0.35 * 1.5) as i32`. Shore (0.35–0.40) generates SAND/ICE blocks at z=0.
    85→
    86→Generation depth: 5 below / 4 above active z-level. Stairs auto-placed at elevation transitions.
    87→
    88→### Surface index & grounding
    89→
    90→Surface index maps `(x, y)` columns to walkable z-levels. Grounding resolution determines movement:
    91→
    92→- Max step up: 1 block
    93→- Fall: unlimited (> 3 blocks = damage, not yet implemented)
    94→- Transitions: Walk, StepUp, StepDown, Fall, Blocked
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Block types
    99→
   100→| ID | Name | ASCII | blocks_movement | supports_standing | Notes |
   101→|----|------|:-----:|:---:|:---:|-------|
   102→| 0 | AIR | `.` | | | Empty space |
   103→| 1 | VOID | ` ` | x | | Impenetrable |
   104→| 2 | HOLE | `o` | | | Vertical shaft, see-through |
   105→| 10 | STONE | `#` | x | x | Solid rock |
   106→| 11 | DIRT | `#` | x | x | Underground fill |
   107→| 20 | GRASS | `#` | | x | Surface terrain |
   108→| 21 | SAND | `#` | | x | Beach / desert |
   109→| 22 | MUD | `#` | | x | Wet terrain |
   110→| 23 | DRY_GRASS | `#` | | x | Arid terrain |
   111→| 24 | SNOW | `#` | | x | Cold terrain |
   112→| 25 | ICE | `#` | x | x | Semi-transparent solid |
   113→| 40 | WALL_STONE | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
   114→| 41 | WALL_WOOD | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
   115→| 42 | GLASS_WALL | `#` | x | | Transparent wall |
   116→| 50 | STAIRS_UP | `^` | | x | Ascending stairs |
   117→| 51 | STAIRS_DOWN | `v` | | x | Descending stairs |
   118→| 60 | DEEP_WATER | `~` | x | | Impassable water |
   119→| 61 | SHALLOW_WATER | `~` | | x | Wadeable |
   120→| 70 | LANTERN | `*` | | | Light source |
   121→
   122→Movement rule: target tile must not have `blocks_movement` at feet level AND must have `supports_standing` below.
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Controls
   127→
   128→### Mode switch
   129→
   130→| Key | Action |
   131→|-----|--------|
   132→| **B** | Toggle Adventure <-> Overseer mode |
   133→
   134→### Adventure mode
   135→
   136→#### Movement
   137→
   138→| Key | Action |
   139→|-----|--------|
   140→| **W/A/S/D** | Move North/West/South/East |
   141→| **Shift** | Sprint |
   142→| **< (Comma)** | Climb stairs up |
   143→| **> (Period)** | Descend stairs down |
   144→
   145→#### Block interaction
   146→
   147→| Input | Action |
   148→|-------|--------|
   149→| **LMB** | Mine block at cursor |
   150→| **RMB** | Place block from hotbar |
   151→| **Ctrl** | Modifier: floor level (z-1) |
   152→| **Alt** | Modifier: ceiling level (z+1) |
   153→| _(no modifier)_ | Wall level (z) |
   154→
   155→Cursor colors: white = wall, blue = floor, orange = ceiling, red = can mine, green = can place.
   156→
   157→#### Hotbar & inventory
   158→
   159→| Input | Action |
   160→|-------|--------|
   161→| **1-0** | Select hotbar slot 1-10 |
   162→| **Scroll wheel** | Cycle hotbar |
   163→| **Tab** | Toggle inventory |
   164→| **Esc** | Close inventory |
   165→| **Q** | Drop item (+Shift = stack, +Ctrl = half) |
   166→| **LMB outside slots** | Drop dragged stack (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |
   167→| **RMB outside slots** | Drop 1 dragged item (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |
   168→
   169→### Overseer mode
   170→
   171→#### Camera
   172→
   173→| Input | Action |
   174→|-------|--------|
   175→| **W/A/S/D** or **Arrows** | Pan camera |
   176→| **MMB drag** | Free pan |
   177→| **Scroll wheel** | Change z-level |
   178→| **PgUp / PgDn** | Jump z-level |
   179→
   180→#### Unit selection
   181→
   182→| Input | Action |
   183→|-------|--------|
   184→| **LMB click** | Select unit (empty = deselect) |
   185→| **LMB drag** | Box select |
   186→| **Shift+LMB** | Toggle / add to selection |
   187→
   188→#### Commands
   189→
   190→| Input | Action |
   191→|-------|--------|
   192→| **RMB** | Issue smart command |
   193→| **Shift+RMB** | Queue command (append) |
   194→| **Esc** | Reset tool to Auto mode |
   195→
   196→Smart action priority: Pickup item > Demolish block > Build (if hotbar has block) > MoveTo.
   197→
   198→Colors: green = move, blue = build, red = demolish, yellow = pickup.
   199→
   200→---
   201→
   202→## TCP debug protocol
   203→
   204→Game listens on `127.0.0.1:9999` (feature `debug-server`, default on). Protocol: JSON lines over TCP — one JSON object + `\n` per command.
   205→
   206→### Response format
   207→
   208→```json
   209→{"id": 1, "ok": true, "data": {...}}
   210→{"id": 2, "ok": false, "data": null, "error": "message"}
   211→```
   212→
   213→### Quick test
   214→
   215→```bash
   216→echo '{"cmd":"Ping"}' | nc -q1 127.0.0.1 9999
   217→```
   218→
   219→### Command reference
   220→
   221→<details>
   222→<summary>Meta</summary>
   223→
   224→| Command | JSON |
   225→|---------|------|
   226→| Ping | `{"cmd":"Ping"}` |
   227→| QueryInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryInfo"}` |
   228→| QueryGameInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryGameInfo"}` |
   229→
   230→</details>
   231→
   232→<details>
   233→<summary>Queries</summary>
   234→
   235→| Command | JSON |
   236→|---------|------|
   237→| QueryState | `{"cmd":"QueryState"}` |
   238→| QueryBlock | `{"cmd":"QueryBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` |
   239→| QuerySurfaces | `{"cmd":"QuerySurfaces","x":N,"y":N}` |
   240→| QueryCanMove | `{"cmd":"QueryCanMove","dir":"North"}` |
   241→| QueryBlocked | `{"cmd":"QueryBlocked"}` |
   242→| QueryLayer | `{"cmd":"QueryLayer","z":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N}` |
   243→| QueryCrossSection | `{"cmd":"QueryCrossSection","y":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` |
   244→| QueryVolume | `{"cmd":"QueryVolume","x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` |
   245→| QueryNearby | `{"cmd":"QueryNearby","radius":N}` |
   246→| QueryFOV | `{"cmd":"QueryFOV","radius":N}` |
   247→
   248→</details>
   249→
   250→<details>
