   317→
   318→## kinswardctl — CLI for game control
   319→
   320→`kinswardctl` is a command-line tool for controlling the running game via TCP debug server. A wrapper over the protocol above with human-readable output.
   321→
   322→### Setup
   323→
   324→```bash
   325→# Game must be running
   326→cargo run
   327→
   328→# In another terminal:
   329→cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- ping
   330→```
   331→
   332→### Global flags
   333→
   334→| Flag | Description |
   335→|------|-------------|
   336→| `--host <IP>` | Server address (default `127.0.0.1`) |
   337→| `--port <PORT>` | Port (default `9999`) |
   338→| `--json` | Raw JSON output |
   339→
   340→```bash
   341→kinswardctl --json state          # raw JSON
   342→kinswardctl --port 8888 ping      # custom port
   343→```
   344→
   345→### Meta
   346→
   347→```bash
   348→kinswardctl ping                  # test connection
   349→kinswardctl info                  # active_z and basic info
   350→kinswardctl game-info             # seed, layers, chunks, entities
   351→```
   352→
   353→### Player state
   354→
   355→```bash
   356→kinswardctl state
   357→# OK  x=0  y=0  z=1  active_z=1  block_at_feet=AIR  block_below=GRASS  has_support=true
   358→
   359→kinswardctl blocked
   360→# OK  blocked: West, North
   361→
   362→kinswardctl can-move north
   363→# OK  North can_move=false
   364→```
   365→
   366→### Movement
   367→
   368→```bash
   369→kinswardctl move north            # one tile (n/s/e/w)
   370→kinswardctl move-delta 3 0        # offset dx=3, dy=0
   371→kinswardctl stairs up             # climb stairs
   372→kinswardctl stairs down           # descend stairs
   373→kinswardctl teleport 10 5 2       # teleport to (10, 5, z=2)
   374→```
   375→
   376→### World visualization
   377→
   378→```bash
   379→# Top-down layer at z=1, x=-5..5, y=-5..5
   380→kinswardctl layer -z 1 -x -5:5 -y -5:5
   381→
   382→# Side cross-section at y=0, x=-5..5, z=0..3
   383→kinswardctl cross-section -y 0 -x -5:5 -z 0:3
   384→
   385→# 3D volume data (JSON)
   386→kinswardctl volume -x -2:2 -y -2:2 -z 0:2
   387→
   388→# Field of view (ASCII visibility map)
   389→kinswardctl fov --radius 12
   390→
   391→# Nearby items
   392→kinswardctl nearby --radius 10
   393→# OK  3 items within radius 10 of (0,0,1)
   394→#   [4294967302] (1,2) Stone x5  dist=2
   395→#   [4294967310] (-1,0) Apple x1  dist=1
   396→```
   397→
   398→### World modification
   399→
   400→```bash
   401→kinswardctl set-block 5 5 1 STONE
   402→# OK  set (5,5,1) to STONE
   403→
   404→kinswardctl fill -x -3:3 -y -3:3 -z 1 AIR
   405→# OK  filled 49 blocks with AIR
   406→
   407→kinswardctl rebuild-surfaces       # usually auto, manual if needed
   408→```
   409→
   410→### Registry
   411→
   412→```bash
   413→kinswardctl registry blocks        # all block types
   414→kinswardctl registry items         # all items
   415→kinswardctl registry floor-covers  # floor covers
   416→
   417→kinswardctl lookup blocks STONE    # lookup by name or id
   418→kinswardctl lookup items apple     # search items (substring, case-insensitive)
   419→kinswardctl lookup items 102       # search by numeric id
   420→```
   421→
   422→### Unit management
   423→
   424→Units execute queued commands autonomously with A* pathfinding. Works in any input mode.
   425→
   426→```bash
   427→# List units (get entity IDs)
   428→kinswardctl units
   429→# OK  2 unit(s)
   430→#   ID                   Name             Position     Queue  Status
   431→#   4294967305           Worker           (3,2,1)      0      idle
   432→
   433→# Detailed info
   434→kinswardctl unit-info 4294967305
   435→# OK  Unit: Worker (id=4294967305)
   436→#   Position: (3,2,1)
   437→#   Status: idle  Hotbar slot: 0
   438→#   Inventory (2 items):
   439→#     [0] Stone Block x10
   440→#     [1] Apple x3
   441→#   Queue: (empty)
   442→
   443→# Move unit to target
   444→kinswardctl unit-move 4294967305 10 5 1
   445→# OK  queued (queue_len=1)
   446→
   447→# Build block (item_id from registry items)
   448→kinswardctl unit-build 4294967305 10 5 1 102
   449→# OK  queued (queue_len=2)
   450→
   451→# Demolish block
   452→kinswardctl unit-demolish 4294967305 10 6 1
   453→
   454→# Pick up item (target_entity_id from nearby)
   455→kinswardctl unit-pickup 4294967305 4294967310
   456→
   457→# Multi-waypoint route (single TCP connection)
   458→kinswardctl unit-plan 4294967305 5,0,1 5,5,1 0,5,1
   459→#   1. 5,0,1 -> queued (queue_len=1)
   460→#   2. 5,5,1 -> queued (queue_len=2)
   461→#   3. 0,5,1 -> queued (queue_len=3)
   462→# OK  3 waypoints queued for unit 4294967305
   463→
   464→# Clear queue (unit finishes current path segment)
   465→kinswardctl unit-queue-clear 4294967305
   466→
   467→# Full stop (immediate)
   468→kinswardctl unit-stop 4294967305
   469→```
   470→
   471→### Task management
   472→
   473→Global task queue decouples task creation from unit assignment. Tasks are auto-assigned to idle units.
   474→
   475→```bash
   476→kinswardctl tasks                                  # list all tasks
   477→kinswardctl tasks --status pending                 # filter: pending/assigned/blocked
   478→kinswardctl task-add demolish 5 5 1                # create task
   479→kinswardctl task-add build 5 5 1 --item 102        # create build task
   480→kinswardctl task-cancel 42                         # cancel task by ID
   481→kinswardctl task-cancel-zone -x 0:5 -y 0:5 -z 1   # cancel zone tasks
   482→```
   483→
   484→### Raw JSON
   485→
   486→```bash
   487→kinswardctl raw '{"cmd":"QueryState"}'
   488→```
   489→
   490→### Help
   491→
   492→```bash
   493→kinswardctl --help                 # all commands
   494→kinswardctl move --help            # help for specific command
   495→```
   496→
   497→### Tips
   498→
   499→- Start with `state` to get player position and surroundings
   500→- `blocked` is more efficient than 4 `can-move` calls
   501→- `layer` and `cross-section` for quick spatial orientation
   502→- `fill` instead of many `set-block` for bulk operations
   503→- `unit-plan` sends multiple waypoints over a single TCP connection
   504→- `unit-stop` for immediate halt; `unit-queue-clear` lets unit finish current path
   505→- `nearby` returns `entity_id` for items — use with `unit-pickup`
   506→- `--json` flag goes before subcommand: `kinswardctl --json state`
   507→

## Repository Exploration Report

I've successfully explored the Kinsward game repository at `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/`. Here's the complete information for your documentation:

### 1. README.md - FOUND
**Location:** `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/README.md`

**Key Contents:**
- **Overview:** 2D voxel-like game with z-levels, built on Bevy 0.18
- **Build & Run Commands:**
  - `cargo run` — clean run (no debug UI)
  - `cargo run -- --debug` — debug mode (admin panel, test items, physics gizmos)
  - `cargo run -- --seed 42` — fixed world seed
  - `cargo run --features dev` — dynamic linking (faster recompilation)
  - `cargo build --no-default-features` — without debug-server and inspector

- **CLI Flags:**
  - `--debug` — Enable debug mode
  - `--seed <N>` — World seed

- **Feature Flags:**
  - `debug` (default) — debug-server + bevy-inspector-egui
  - `debug-server` — TCP server on `127.0.0.1:9999`
  - `dev` — debug + dynamic linking

- **Architecture - Coordinate System:**
  - X = East (+) / West (-)
  - Y = North (+) / South (-)
  - Z = vertical levels (up +)
  - Tile size: 32 px (TILE_SIZE_PX)
  - Player starts at (0, 0, z=1) on GRASS at z=0

- **World Model:**
  ```
  WorldMap
    └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>
       └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk> (32x32)
          ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>
          ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>
          └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>
  ```

- **Generation (Noise-based FBM Perlin):**
  - Elevation (6 octaves, 0.008 scale)
  - Moisture (4 octaves, 0.012 scale)
  - Temperature (1 octave, 0.006 scale)
  - Cave (3 octaves, 0.05 scale)

- **Block Types** (21 types including AIR, VOID, HOLE, STONE, DIRT, GRASS, SAND, MUD, DRY_GRASS, SNOW, ICE, WALL_STONE, WALL_WOOD, GLASS_WALL, STAIRS_UP, STAIRS_DOWN, DEEP_WATER, SHALLOW_WATER, LANTERN)

- **Controls:** Detailed controls for Adventure mode (W/A/S/D movement, LMB mining, RMB placement) and Overseer mode (camera control, unit selection, zone commands)

- **TCP Debug Protocol:** Runs on `127.0.0.1:9999`, JSON lines format with response: `{"id": N, "ok": bool, "data": {...}}`

---

### 2. CLAUDE.md - FOUND
**Location:** `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/CLAUDE.md`

**Key Contents:**

- **Build Commands:**
  - `cargo run` — default (debug features)
  - `cargo run --features dev` — dynamic linking for faster recompilation
  - `cargo build --no-default-features` — release-like build
  - `cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- <cmd>` — CLI debug tool
  - `cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42` — headless worldgen

- **Testing:**
  ```bash
  cargo test
  cargo test <test_name>
  cargo test --lib world::chunk
  ```
  Tests are inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` within source files, primarily in `src/world/`

- **Architecture:**
  - **Engine:** Bevy 0.18 ECS + avian2d physics
  - **System execution order (GameSet):**
    ```
    Input -> Commands -> Movement -> Fov -> Lighting -> Barriers -> Render
    ```

- **World Model:**
  - `CHUNK_SIZE = 32`, `TILE_SIZE_PX = 32.0`
  - `BlockType` is u16 newtype (not enum) with named constants
  - `BlockRegistry` uses flat `Vec<Option<BlockProps>>` indexed by u16 for O(1) lookup
  - Block definitions from `assets/blocks.ron` (RON with IMPLICIT_SOME)
  - Item definitions from `assets/items.ron`

- **Climate Pipeline (16 steps in `src/generation/climate/`):**
  1. Plates + Elevation (Voronoi plates, boundary uplift)
  2. Terrain Conditioning (thermal erosion, smoothing)
  3. Sea Level (ocean/coast/land BFS)
  4. Temperature (latitude + lapse rate + ocean proximity)
  5. Pressure (Hadley + thermal + barometric + noise)
  6. Wind (pressure gradient + Coriolis + terrain deflection)
  7. Rainfall (evaporation + advection + orographic)
  8. Geology (rock types: 8 RockType enum variants)
  9. Depression Fill (priority-flood)
  10. Drainage (elevation + slope + rock permeability)
  11. Proto-Rivers (flow accumulation + lake filling)
  12. Fluvial Erosion (river valleys, rock hardness modulation)
  13. Final Rivers (recompute on eroded terrain)
  14. Biomes (temperature × rainfall × elevation × drainage, 22 BiomeId variants)
  15. Fertility (soil fertility from rain + temp + drainage + elevation + rivers)
  16. Vegetation (plant density from biome + rainfall + temperature + noise)

- **Key Patterns:**
  - **Dirty flags** (`DirtyFlags` resource) — tracks what needs recomputation
  - **Command system** (`src/commands/`) — `GameCommand` enum with serde-tagged commands, routed through CommandQueue
  - **Task system** (`src/overseer/task_queue.rs`) — Global TaskQueue with Build/Demolish/Pickup tasks
  - **State machine** (`src/overseer/mode.rs`) — InputMode (Adventure | Overseer)
  - **UnitZ component** — Tracks z-level independently from Transform
  - **Surface index** (`src/world/surface_index.rs`) — Maps (x,y) columns to walkable z-levels

- **Rendering:**
  - Tile-based: one Bevy entity per visible tile
  - Sprites from 10px-cell texture atlases (Natural.png, Civilized.png, People.png) scaled 3.2×
  - Final tile color = base_color × depth_factor × light_value × visibility

- **Module Layout:**
  - `src/world/` — WorldMap, blocks, chunks, layers, surfaces, grounding
  - `src/commands/` — GameCommand, executor, TCP server, region-map export
  - `src/render/` — Viewport, sprites, FPS display
  - `src/fov/` — Shadowcasting FOV, visibility maps
  - `src/lighting/` — Light propagation
  - `src/generation/` — Procedural generation, climate pipeline
  - `src/units/` — Unit components, spawner, possession
  - `src/overseer/` — RTS mode: camera, selection, commands, pathfinding, task queue
  - `src/companions/` — Companion behavior
  - `src/ui/` — UI animation system

### 3. Cargo.toml - FOUND
**Location:** `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/Cargo.toml`

**Key Details:**
- **Package:** `kinsward`

**Binaries:**
1. `kinsward` — main game (src/main.rs)

### 4. src/bin/kinswardctl.rs - FOUND
**Location:** `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/bin/kinswardctl.rs`

**CLI Structure:**
```rust
Cli {
    host: String (default "127.0.0.1")
    port: u16 (default 9999)
    json: bool (--json flag)
    command: Cmd (subcommand enum)
}
```

**Subcommand Groups (Cmd enum):**

**Movement:**
- `Move { dir: String }` — cardinal direction
- `MoveDelta { dx: i32, dy: i32 }` — tile offset
- `Stairs { direction: String }` — "up" or "down"
- `Teleport { x, y, z }`

**Queries:**
- `State` — player state
- `Block { x, y, z }` — block info
- `Surfaces { x, y }` — walkable z-levels
- `CanMove { dir }` — can player move?
- `Blocked` — all blocked directions
- `CrossSection { y, x_range, z_range }` — XZ side view
- `Layer { z, x_range, y_range }` — XY top-down view
- `Volume { x_range, y_range, z_range }` — 3D volume snapshot
- `Nearby { radius }` — items near player
- `Fov { radius }` — field of view visibility map

**Inventory:**
- `PickupNearest` — pick up nearest item
- `DropFromSlot { slot, count }` — drop items
- `SelectHotbar { slot }` — select hotbar slot

**World Modification:**
- `MineBlock { x, y, z }` — remove block
- `PlaceBlock { x, y, z, item_id }` — place block from inventory
- `SetBlock { x, y, z, block_type }` — set block type
- `Fill { x_range, y_range, z, block_type }` — fill area
- `RebuildSurfaces` — rebuild surface index

**Registry:**
- `Registry { kind }` — list all entries (blocks/items/floor-covers)
- `Lookup { kind, query }` — search by name or id
- `Info` — server info
- `GameInfo` — extended game info

**Units:**
- `Units` — list all units
- `UnitInfo { entity_id }` — detailed unit info
- `UnitMove { entity_id, x, y, z }` — queue move
- `UnitBuild { entity_id, x, y, z, item_id }` — queue build
- `UnitDemolish { entity_id, x, y, z }` — queue demolish
- `UnitPickup { entity_id, target_entity_id }` — queue pickup
- `UnitQueueClear { entity_id }` — clear queue
- `UnitStop { entity_id }` — immediate stop
- `UnitPlan { entity_id, waypoints }` — multiple waypoints
- `UnitCancelTask { entity_id, tile?, index? }` — cancel specific task
- `UnitFollow { follower_id, target_id }` — make unit follow another

**Spawning:**
- `Spawn { kind, x, y, z, name?, faction?, controlled? }` — spawn unit
- `Possess { entity_id? }` — possess/release unit

**Zone Operations:**
- `ZoneDemolish { units, x_range, y_range, z, append? }` — bulk demolish
- `ZoneBuild { units, x_range, y_range, z, item, append? }` — bulk build
- `ZoneCancel { units?, x_range, y_range, z }` — cancel zone tasks

**Companions:**
- `CompanionList` — list companions
- `CompanionBehavior { entity_id, behavior }` — set behavior
- `CompanionCallAll` — call all (Follow)
- `CompanionDismissAll` — dismiss all (Station)

**Tasks:**
- `Tasks { status? }` — list tasks (all/pending/assigned/blocked)
- `TaskAdd { kind, x, y, z, item?, target? }` — create task
- `TaskCancel { task_id }` — cancel task
- `TaskCancelZone { x_range, y_range, z }` — cancel zone tasks

**Region Map:**
- `RegionMap { layer, format?, path?, x?, y?, size?, width? }` — export map layers
  - Layers: elevation, temperature, rainfall, wind, drainage, rivers, geology, fertility, vegetation, plates, biome
  - Formats: png, ascii, stats

**Meta:**
- `Ping` — test connection
- `Raw { json_str }` — send raw JSON

### 5. .claude/skills/game-control.md - FOUND
**Location:** `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/.claude/skills/game-control.md`

This is a **comprehensive TCP protocol and CLI reference** (348 lines). It documents:

**Connection Details:**
- TCP server on `127.0.0.1:9999` (enabled by default with `debug-server` feature)
- JSON lines protocol (each command is a JSON object + `\n`)
- Response format: `{"id": N, "ok": true, "data": {...}}` or `{"id": N, "ok": false, "error": "..."}`

**Command Categories with Full JSON Examples:**

1. **Meta Commands (3):** Ping, QueryInfo, QueryGameInfo
2. **Registry Commands (3):** QueryRegistry, QueryRegistryLookup (blocks/items/floor_covers)
3. **Query Commands (9):** QueryState, QueryBlock, QuerySurfaces, QueryCanMove, QueryBlocked, QueryLayer, QueryCrossSection, QueryVolume, QueryNearby, QueryFOV
4. **Movement Commands (5):** MoveDir, MoveDelta, StairsUp, StairsDown, Teleport
5. **World Modification (4):** SetBlock, FillArea, MineBlock, PlaceBlock, RebuildSurfaces
6. **Inventory (3):** PickupNearest, DropFromSlot, SelectHotbar
7. **Unit Management (9):** QueryUnits, QueryUnitInfo, UnitMoveTo, UnitBuild, UnitDemolish, UnitPickup, UnitQueueClear, UnitStop, UnitCancelTask, UnitFollow

- **Climate Pipeline (16 steps in `src/generation/climate/`):**
  1. Plates + Elevation (Voronoi plates, boundary uplift)
  2. Terrain Conditioning (thermal erosion, smoothing)
  3. Sea Level (ocean/coast/land BFS)
  4. Temperature (latitude + lapse rate + ocean proximity)
  5. Pressure (Hadley + thermal + barometric + noise)
  6. Wind (pressure gradient + Coriolis + terrain deflection)
  7. Rainfall (evaporation + advection + orographic)
  8. Geology (rock types: 8 RockType enum variants)
  9. Depression Fill (priority-flood)
  10. Drainage (elevation + slope + rock permeability)
  11. Proto-Rivers (flow accumulation + lake filling)
  12. Fluvial Erosion (river valleys, rock hardness modulation)
  13. Final Rivers (recompute on eroded terrain)
  14. Biomes (temperature × rainfall × elevation × drainage, 22 BiomeId variants)
  15. Fertility (soil fertility from rain + temp + drainage + elevation + rivers)
  16. Vegetation (plant density from biome + rainfall + temperature + noise)

**Adventure Mode Controls (Implemented):**
- **Movement:** W/A/S/D (cardinal), Shift (sprint)
- **Stairs:** < (up), > (down)
- **Block Interaction:** LMB (mine), RMB (place), Ctrl/Alt modifiers (z-level view override)
- **Hotbar:** 1-0 (select slot), Scroll wheel (cycle), Q (drop with modifiers)
- **Inventory:** Tab (toggle), Esc (close)

**Overseer Mode Controls (Implemented):**
- **Camera:** W/A/S/D or Arrow keys (pan), MMB drag (free pan), Scroll wheel (z-level), PageUp/PageDn (jump z)
- **Selection:** LMB (select), LMB drag (box select), Shift+LMB (toggle)
- **Commands:** RMB (smart action), Shift+RMB (queue)
- **Mode Toggle:** B key (Adventure ↔ Overseer)

**Special Keys:**
- `KeyCode::Escape` - Cancel mode, close UI
- Number keys (1-0) - Reactivate hotbar, select items
- Shift modifiers - Command queuing, inventory fine control

---

## 2. GAME SYSTEMS & EXECUTION ORDER

**File:** `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/schedule.rs`

**System Sets (GameSet enum):**
```rust
pub enum GameSet {
    Input,           // WASD, mouse, keyboard
    Commands,        // TCP, overseer commands
    Movement,        // Player/unit movement, stairs, terrain sync
    Fov,            // Field of view computation
    Lighting,       // Lighting (after FOV)
    Barriers,       // Elevation barriers (after FOV + movement)
    Render,         // Tile rendering (after lighting + FOV)
}
```

**Execution Order:**
1. Input → Commands → Movement (in parallel after Input)
2. Fov (after Movement)
3. Lighting & Barriers (after Fov)
4. Render (after Lighting + Barriers)

- `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/world/layer.rs` - Z-level layers
- `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/world/map.rs` - WorldMap

**Hierarchy:**
```
WorldMap
  ├─ HashMap<i32, Layer>        (z-level → Layer)
  │  ├─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>   (chunk pos → 32x32 Chunk)
  │  ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>         (dense block storage)
  │  ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    (sparse)
  │  └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  (sparse)
```

**Constants:**
- `CHUNK_SIZE = 32x32` tiles
- `TILE_SIZE_PX = 32.0` pixels
- `Z-range: -100 to +100`
- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1

1. **Elevation** (6 octaves, scale 0.008) → surface z-level

< 0.55 → z=0  (plains)
< 0.70 → z=1  (hills)
< 0.85 → z=2  (mountains)
≥ 0.85 → z=3  (peaks)
```

file.content:
name: Release Build

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+*"

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  BINARY_NAME: kinsward
  VERSION: ${{ github.ref_name }}

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  build:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            suffix: linux-x86_64
            binary_ext: ""
          - os: windows-latest
            target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            suffix: windows-x86_64
            binary_ext: ".exe"

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install Rust toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          key: ${{ matrix.target }}

      # Linux: install Bevy system dependencies
      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y \
            pkg-config \
            libx11-dev \
            libasound2-dev \
            libudev-dev \
            libxkbcommon-x11-dev \
            libwayland-dev \
            libxkbcommon-dev \
            libvulkan-dev

      - name: Build release
        run: cargo build --release --no-default-features

      - name: Prepare artifact (Linux)
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          mkdir -p artifact
          cp target/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }} artifact/
          cp -r assets artifact/ 2>/dev/null || true
          cd artifact
          tar czf ../${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.tar.gz *

      - name: Prepare artifact (Windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path artifact
          Copy-Item "target/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}.exe" artifact/
          if (Test-Path "assets") { Copy-Item -Recurse "assets" artifact/ }
          Compress-Archive -Path artifact/* -DestinationPath "${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.zip"

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}
          path: |
            ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.tar.gz
            ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.zip
          if-no-files-found: ignore

  release:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Download all artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            artifacts/*.tar.gz
            artifacts/*.zip

     4→
     5→## Build & Run
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→cargo run                              # clean run (no debug UI)
     9→cargo run -- --debug                   # debug mode (admin panel, test items, physics gizmos)
    10→cargo run -- --seed 42                 # fixed world seed
    11→cargo run -- --debug --seed 42         # both
    12→DEBUG=true cargo run                   # debug mode via env var
    13→cargo run --features dev               # dynamic linking (faster recompilation)
    14→cargo build --no-default-features      # without debug-server and inspector
    15→```
    16→
    17→### CLI flags
    18→
    19→| Flag | Description |
    20→|------|-------------|
    21→| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
    22→| `--seed <N>` | World seed (overrides `WORLD_SEED` env var) |
    23→
    24→Env vars: `DEBUG=true` — same as `--debug`. `WORLD_SEED=N` — fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority).
    25→
    26→### Feature flags
    27→
    28→| Feature | Description |
    29→|---------|-------------|
    30→| `debug` (default) | debug-server + bevy-inspector-egui |
    31→| `debug-server` | TCP server on `127.0.0.1:9999` |
    32→| `dev` | debug + dynamic linking (fast iteration) |
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Architecture
    37→
    38→### Coordinate system
    39→
    40→- **X** = East (+) / West (-)
    41→- **Y** = North (+) / South (-)
    42→- **Z** = vertical levels (up +)
    43→- Tile size: 32 px (`TILE_SIZE_PX`)
    44→- Player starts at (0, 0, z=1) on GRASS at z=0
    45→
    46→### World model
    47→
    48→```
    49→WorldMap
    50→  └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>           z-level → Layer
    51→     └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>      chunk pos → Chunk (32x32)
    52→        ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>        dense block storage
    53→        ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    sparse floor covers
    54→        └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  sparse ceiling covers
    55→```
    56→
    57→- **BlockVolume** wraps `BlockType` (u16), occupies z to z+1
    58→- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1
    59→- `block_at_feet` — block at player's z (usually AIR)
    60→- `block_below` — block under player (usually solid terrain)
    61→
    62→### Generation
    63→
    64→Noise-based procedural generation (FBM Perlin):
    65→
    66→| Noise | Octaves | Scale | Determines |
    67→|-------|---------|-------|------------|
    68→| Elevation | 6 | 0.008 | Terrain shape, z-level |
    69→| Moisture | 4 | 0.012 | Biome (grass/mud/sand) |
    70→| Temperature | 1 | 0.006 | Climate (snow/ice) |
    71→| Cave | 3 | 0.05 | Underground caverns |
    72→
    73→Elevation → surface z-level:
    74→
    75→| Elevation | Z | Terrain |
    76→|-----------|---|---------|

    78→| < 0.35 | -1 | Shallow water |
    79→| < 0.55 | 0 | Plains |
    80→| < 0.70 | 1 | Hills |

    82→| >= 0.85 | 3 | Peaks |
    83→

    85→

    87→
    88→### Surface index & grounding
    89→
    90→Surface index maps `(x, y)` columns to walkable z-levels. Grounding resolution determines movement:
    91→
    92→- Max step up: 1 block
    93→- Fall: unlimited (> 3 blocks = damage, not yet implemented)
    94→- Transitions: Walk, StepUp, StepDown, Fall, Blocked
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Block types
    99→
   100→| ID | Name | ASCII | blocks_movement | supports_standing | Notes |

file.content:
# Kinsward

2D voxel-like game with z-levels, built on Bevy 0.18.

## Build & Run

```bash
cargo run                              # clean run (no debug UI)
cargo run -- --debug                   # debug mode (admin panel, test items, physics gizmos)
cargo run -- --seed 42                 # fixed world seed
cargo run -- --debug --seed 42         # both
DEBUG=true cargo run                   # debug mode via env var
cargo run --features dev               # dynamic linking (faster recompilation)
cargo build --no-default-features      # without debug-server and inspector
```

### CLI flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
| `--seed <N>` | World seed (overrides `WORLD_SEED` env var) |

Env vars: `DEBUG=true` — same as `--debug`. `WORLD_SEED=N` — fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority).

### Feature flags

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `debug` (default) | debug-server + bevy-inspector-egui |
| `debug-server` | TCP server on `127.0.0.1:9999` |
| `dev` | debug + dynamic linking (fast iteration) |

---

## Architecture

### Coordinate system

- **X** = East (+) / West (-)
- **Y** = North (+) / South (-)
- **Z** = vertical levels (up +)
- Tile size: 32 px (`TILE_SIZE_PX`)
- Player starts at (0, 0, z=1) on GRASS at z=0

### World model

```
WorldMap
  └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>           z-level → Layer
     └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>      chunk pos → Chunk (32x32)
        ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>        dense block storage
        ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    sparse floor covers
        └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  sparse ceiling covers
```

- **BlockVolume** wraps `BlockType` (u16), occupies z to z+1
- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1
- `block_at_feet` — block at player's z (usually AIR)
- `block_below` — block under player (usually solid terrain)

### Generation

Noise-based procedural generation (FBM Perlin):

| Noise | Octaves | Scale | Determines |
|-------|---------|-------|------------|
| Elevation | 6 | 0.008 | Terrain shape, z-level |
| Moisture | 4 | 0.012 | Biome (grass/mud/sand) |
| Temperature | 1 | 0.006 | Climate (snow/ice) |
| Cave | 3 | 0.05 | Underground caverns |

Elevation → surface z-level:

| Elevation | Z | Terrain |
|-----------|---|---------|
| < ~0.12 | -2 | Deep water |
| < 0.35 | -1 | Shallow water |
| < 0.55 | 0 | Plains |
| < 0.70 | 1 | Hills |
| < 0.85 | 2 | Mountains |
| >= 0.85 | 3 | Peaks |

Water z-level uses a continuous formula: `z = -1 - ((0.35 - elevation) / 0.35 * 1.5) as i32`. Shore (0.35–0.40) generates SAND/ICE blocks at z=0.

Generation depth: 5 below / 4 above active z-level. Stairs auto-placed at elevation transitions.

### Surface index & grounding

Surface index maps `(x, y)` columns to walkable z-levels. Grounding resolution determines movement:

- Max step up: 1 block
- Fall: unlimited (> 3 blocks = damage, not yet implemented)
- Transitions: Walk, StepUp, StepDown, Fall, Blocked

---

## Block types

| ID | Name | ASCII | blocks_movement | supports_standing | Notes |
|----|------|:-----:|:---:|:---:|-------|
| 0 | AIR | `.` | | | Empty space |
| 1 | VOID | ` ` | x | | Impenetrable |
| 2 | HOLE | `o` | | | Vertical shaft, see-through |
| 10 | STONE | `#` | x | x | Solid rock |
| 11 | DIRT | `#` | x | x | Underground fill |
| 20 | GRASS | `#` | | x | Surface terrain |
| 21 | SAND | `#` | | x | Beach / desert |
| 22 | MUD | `#` | | x | Wet terrain |
| 23 | DRY_GRASS | `#` | | x | Arid terrain |
| 24 | SNOW | `#` | | x | Cold terrain |
| 25 | ICE | `#` | x | x | Semi-transparent solid |
| 40 | WALL_STONE | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
| 41 | WALL_WOOD | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
| 42 | GLASS_WALL | `#` | x | | Transparent wall |
| 50 | STAIRS_UP | `^` | | x | Ascending stairs |
| 51 | STAIRS_DOWN | `v` | | x | Descending stairs |
| 60 | DEEP_WATER | `~` | x | | Impassable water |
| 61 | SHALLOW_WATER | `~` | | x | Wadeable |
| 70 | LANTERN | `*` | | | Light source |

Movement rule: target tile must not have `blocks_movement` at feet level AND must have `supports_standing` below.

---

## Controls

### Mode switch

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| **B** | Toggle Adventure <-> Overseer mode |

### Adventure mode

#### Movement

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| **W/A/S/D** | Move North/West/South/East |
| **Shift** | Sprint |
| **< (Comma)** | Climb stairs up |
| **> (Period)** | Descend stairs down |

#### Block interaction

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **LMB** | Mine block at cursor |
| **RMB** | Place block from hotbar |

---
title: Welcome to Starlight
description: Get started building your docs site with Starlight.
template: splash # Remove or comment out this line to display the site sidebar on this page.
hero:
  tagline: Congrats on setting up a new Starlight project!
  image:
    file: ../../assets/houston.webp
  actions:
    - text: Example Guide
      link: /guides/example/
      icon: right-arrow
    - text: Read the Starlight docs
      link: https://starlight.astro.build
      icon: external
      variant: minimal
---

import { Card, CardGrid } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

## Next steps

<CardGrid stagger>
	<Card title="Update content" icon="pencil">
		Edit `src/content/docs/index.mdx` to see this page change.
	</Card>
	<Card title="Change page layout" icon="document">
		Delete `template: splash` in `src/content/docs/index.mdx` to display a
		sidebar on this page.
	</Card>
	<Card title="Add new content" icon="add-document">
		Add Markdown or MDX files to `src/content/docs` to create new pages.
	</Card>
	<Card title="Configure your site" icon="setting">
		Edit your `sidebar` and other config in `astro.config.mjs`.

file.content:
| **RMB** | Place block from hotbar |
| **Ctrl** | Modifier: floor level (z-1) |
| **Alt** | Modifier: ceiling level (z+1) |
| _(no modifier)_ | Wall level (z) |

Cursor colors: white = wall, blue = floor, orange = ceiling, red = can mine, green = can place.

#### Hotbar & inventory

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **1-0** | Select hotbar slot 1-10 |
| **Scroll wheel** | Cycle hotbar |
| **Tab** | Toggle inventory |
| **Esc** | Close inventory |
| **Q** | Drop item (+Shift = stack, +Ctrl = half) |
| **LMB outside slots** | Drop dragged stack (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |
| **RMB outside slots** | Drop 1 dragged item (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |

### Overseer mode

#### Camera

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **W/A/S/D** or **Arrows** | Pan camera |
| **MMB drag** | Free pan |
| **Scroll wheel** | Change z-level |
| **PgUp / PgDn** | Jump z-level |

#### Unit selection

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **LMB click** | Select unit (empty = deselect) |
| **LMB drag** | Box select |
| **Shift+LMB** | Toggle / add to selection |

#### Commands

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **RMB** | Issue smart command |
| **Shift+RMB** | Queue command (append) |
| **Esc** | Reset tool to Auto mode |

Smart action priority: Pickup item > Demolish block > Build (if hotbar has block) > MoveTo.

Colors: green = move, blue = build, red = demolish, yellow = pickup.

---

## TCP debug protocol

Game listens on `127.0.0.1:9999` (feature `debug-server`, default on). Protocol: JSON lines over TCP — one JSON object + `\n` per command.

### Response format

```json
{"id": 1, "ok": true, "data": {...}}
{"id": 2, "ok": false, "data": null, "error": "message"}
```

### Quick test

```bash
echo '{"cmd":"Ping"}' | nc -q1 127.0.0.1 9999
```

### Command reference

<details>
<summary>Meta</summary>

| Command | JSON |
|---------|------|
| Ping | `{"cmd":"Ping"}` |
| QueryInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryInfo"}` |
| QueryGameInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryGameInfo"}` |

</details>

<details>
<summary>Queries</summary>

| Command | JSON |
|---------|------|
| QueryState | `{"cmd":"QueryState"}` |
| QueryBlock | `{"cmd":"QueryBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` |
| QuerySurfaces | `{"cmd":"QuerySurfaces","x":N,"y":N}` |
| QueryCanMove | `{"cmd":"QueryCanMove","dir":"North"}` |
| QueryBlocked | `{"cmd":"QueryBlocked"}` |
| QueryLayer | `{"cmd":"QueryLayer","z":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N}` |
| QueryCrossSection | `{"cmd":"QueryCrossSection","y":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` |
| QueryVolume | `{"cmd":"QueryVolume","x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` |
| QueryNearby | `{"cmd":"QueryNearby","radius":N}` |
| QueryFOV | `{"cmd":"QueryFOV","radius":N}` |

</details>

Design a detailed implementation plan for setting up the kinsward-site repository — an Astro Starlight documentation site for the Kinsward game.

## Current State

The repo at `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward-site/` already has:
- `package.json` — with astro@^6.0.1, @astrojs/starlight@^0.38.2, sharp@^0.34.2, starlight-kbd@^0.4.0, pnpm as package manager
- `astro.config.mjs` — default Starlight template with starlightKbd plugin (array-based social config format for Starlight 0.38)
- `tsconfig.json` — extends astro/tsconfigs/strict
- `.gitignore` — standard Astro gitignore
- `src/content/docs/index.mdx` — default Starlight welcome page
- `src/content/docs/guides/example.md` and `reference/example.md` — default examples
- `src/content.config.ts` — content collection schema
- `src/assets/houston.webp` — default Starlight image
- `public/favicon.svg` — default Starlight favicon
- `node_modules/` — dependencies already installed via pnpm
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` — untracked
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` — modified

NO `.github/workflows/`, NO `src/styles/`, NO custom logos.

The game repo at `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/` has:
- Version: 0.17.0 (from Cargo.toml)
- Binary names: `kinsward` (main), `kinswardctl` (CLI, source file is src/bin/kinswardctl.rs), `worldgen`
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists and needs a `publish-to-site` job added

## Key Differences from User's Task Spec

1. **Package versions**: The existing package.json has astro@^6.0.1 and starlight@^0.38.2 (newer than task's ^5.7/^0.34). Keep existing newer versions.
2. **Social config format**: Starlight 0.38 uses array format `social: [{ icon: 'github', label: 'GitHub', href: '...' }]` not the old object format `social: { github: '...' }`. Must use the new format.
3. **Package manager**: pnpm is used, not npm. Use `pnpm install` and `pnpm run build`, and in CI use pnpm.
4. **Binary name**: The CLI tool source is already `src/bin/kinswardctl.rs` (not gamectl.rs as task assumes). So the binary is already `kinswardctl` in source too. The task mentions `gamectl` → `kinswardctl` rename, but it's already kinswardctl.
5. **Artifact naming in release.yml**: The existing release workflow uses `BINARY_NAME: kinsward`, so artifacts are already named `kinsward-*`. The publish-to-site job doesn't need to rename from `my_game` since it's already `kinsward`.

## Implementation Plan Required

Design the full implementation covering:

### Files to modify:
1. `astro.config.mjs` — full rewrite with site URL, sidebar, logo, customCss, description
2. `.gitignore` — add IDE files patterns

### Files to create:
1. `src/assets/logo-dark.svg` — light green "K" on transparent
2. `src/assets/logo-light.svg` — dark green "K" on transparent  
3. `public/favicon.svg` — "K" on dark rounded rect (overwrite existing)
4. `src/styles/custom.css` — dark game theme with green accents, IBM Plex fonts
5. `src/env.d.ts` — Astro types reference
6. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — GitHub Pages deployment (using pnpm, not npm)

### Content files to create (all under src/content/docs/):
7. `index.mdx` — splash landing page (overwrite existing)
8. `getting-started/download.md`
9. `getting-started/installation.md`
10. `getting-started/controls.mdx` — uses <Kbd> component
11. `gameplay/adventure-mode.md` (or .mdx if keyboard shortcuts mentioned)
12. `gameplay/overseer-mode.md` (or .mdx if keyboard shortcuts mentioned)
13. `gameplay/world-generation.md`
14. `gameplay/units.md`
15. `gameplay/crafting.md`
16. `gamectl/overview.md`
17. `gamectl/commands.md`
18. `gamectl/examples.md`
19. `development/roadmap.md`
20. `development/changelog.md`
21. `development/architecture.md`

### Files to delete:
- `src/content/docs/guides/example.md`
- `src/content/docs/reference/example.md`
- `src/assets/houston.webp`

### In the game repo:
22. `.github/workflows/release.yml` — add `publish-to-site` job

### README:
23. `README.md` — overwrite with project description

## Source Data for Content

**Game version**: 0.17.0
**Engine**: Bevy 0.18, avian2d physics
**Binaries**: kinsward, kinswardctl, worldgen

**Controls (from README.md)**:
- Adventure: W/A/S/D move, Shift sprint, < stairs up, > stairs down, LMB mine, RMB place, Ctrl/Alt z-level modifiers, 1-0 hotbar, Scroll cycle, Tab inventory, Esc close, Q drop
- Overseer: W/A/S/D/Arrows pan, MMB drag pan, Scroll z-level, PgUp/PgDn jump z, LMB select, LMB drag box select, Shift+LMB toggle, RMB smart command, Shift+RMB queue, Esc reset tool
- Mode switch: B toggle

**kinswardctl commands** (40+ subcommands): All documented in the exploration - movement, queries, inventory, world modification, registry, units, spawning, zone ops, companions, tasks, region map, meta

**Architecture**: GameSet execution order (Input→Commands→Movement→Fov→Lighting→Barriers→Render), WorldMap structure, BlockType u16, RON data-driven design

**World gen**: 16-step climate pipeline with elevation, moisture, temperature, caves, rainfall, drainage, rivers, biomes (22 types), fertility, vegetation, geology

**Implemented features**: Adventure mode, overseer mode, block interaction, unit commands, A* pathfinding, inventory/hotbar, world generation, FOV, lighting, stairs/z-levels, companions, task system, TCP debug protocol

**NOT implemented**: Crafting (zero code), non-player AI (planned), fall damage (planned), ramps/ladders (planned)

**Block types**: 21+ types (Air, Void, Hole, Stone, Dirt, Grass, Sand, Mud, Dry Grass, Snow, Ice, Stone Wall, Wood Wall, Glass Wall, Stairs Up/Down, Deep/Shallow Water, Lantern, Tree Trunk, Leaves, Bush, Tall Grass)

**Items**: Apple, Wood (food/resources), block items (100+)

Please provide a complete, ordered implementation plan with all file contents considerations. Note that pages mentioning keyboard shortcuts should be .mdx with <Kbd> imports.

**Implemented features**: Adventure mode, overseer mode, block interaction, unit commands, A* pathfinding, inventory/hotbar, world generation, FOV, lighting, stairs/z-levels, companions, task system, TCP debug protocol

**NOT implemented**: Crafting (zero code), non-player AI (planned), fall damage (planned), ramps/ladders (planned)

**Block types**: 21+ types (Air, Void, Hole, Stone, Dirt, Grass, Sand, Mud, Dry Grass, Snow, Ice, Stone Wall, Wood Wall, Glass Wall, Stairs Up/Down, Deep/Shallow Water, Lantern, Tree Trunk, Leaves, Bush, Tall Grass)

**Items**: Apple, Wood (food/resources), block items (100+)

Please provide a complete, ordered implementation plan with all file contents considerations. Note that pages mentioning keyboard shortcuts should be .mdx with <Kbd> imports.

1→# Starlight Starter Kit: Basics
     2→
     3→[![Built with Starlight](https://astro.badg.es/v2/built-with-starlight/tiny.svg)](https://starlight.astro.build)
     4→
     5→```
     6→pnpm create astro@latest -- --template starlight
     7→```
     8→
     9→> 🧑‍🚀 **Seasoned astronaut?** Delete this file. Have fun!
    10→
    11→## 🚀 Project Structure
    12→
    13→Inside of your Astro + Starlight project, you'll see the following folders and files:
    14→
    15→```
    16→.
    17→├── public/
    18→├── src/
    19→│   ├── assets/
    20→│   ├── content/
    21→│   │   └── docs/
    22→│   └── content.config.ts
    23→├── astro.config.mjs
    24→├── package.json
    25→└── tsconfig.json
    26→```
    27→
    28→Starlight looks for `.md` or `.mdx` files in the `src/content/docs/` directory. Each file is exposed as a route based on its file name.
    29→
    30→Images can be added to `src/assets/` and embedded in Markdown with a relative link.
    31→
    32→Static assets, like favicons, can be placed in the `public/` directory.
    33→
    34→## 🧞 Commands
    35→
    36→All commands are run from the root of the project, from a terminal:
    37→
    38→| Command                   | Action                                           |
    39→| :------------------------ | :----------------------------------------------- |
    40→| `pnpm install`             | Installs dependencies                            |
    41→| `pnpm dev`             | Starts local dev server at `localhost:4321`      |
    42→| `pnpm build`           | Build your production site to `./dist/`          |
    43→| `pnpm preview`         | Preview your build locally, before deploying     |
    44→| `pnpm astro ...`       | Run CLI commands like `astro add`, `astro check` |
    45→| `pnpm astro -- --help` | Get help using the Astro CLI                     |
    46→
    47→## 👀 Want to learn more?
    48→
    49→Check out [Starlight’s docs](https://starlight.astro.build/), read [the Astro documentation](https://docs.astro.build), or jump into the [Astro Discord server](https://astro.build/chat).
    50→

1→# Kinsward
     2→
     3→2D voxel-like game with z-levels, built on Bevy 0.18.
     4→
     5→## Build & Run
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→cargo run                              # clean run (no debug UI)
     9→cargo run -- --debug                   # debug mode (admin panel, test items, physics gizmos)
    10→cargo run -- --seed 42                 # fixed world seed
    11→cargo run -- --debug --seed 42         # both
    12→DEBUG=true cargo run                   # debug mode via env var
    13→cargo run --features dev               # dynamic linking (faster recompilation)
    14→cargo build --no-default-features      # without debug-server and inspector
    15→```
    16→
    17→### CLI flags
    18→
    19→| Flag | Description |
    20→|------|-------------|
    21→| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
    22→| `--seed <N>` | World seed (overrides `WORLD_SEED` env var) |
    23→
    24→Env vars: `DEBUG=true` — same as `--debug`. `WORLD_SEED=N` — fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority).
    25→
    26→### Feature flags
    27→
    28→| Feature | Description |
    29→|---------|-------------|
    30→| `debug` (default) | debug-server + bevy-inspector-egui |
    31→| `debug-server` | TCP server on `127.0.0.1:9999` |
    32→| `dev` | debug + dynamic linking (fast iteration) |
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Architecture
    37→
    38→### Coordinate system
    39→
    40→- **X** = East (+) / West (-)
    41→- **Y** = North (+) / South (-)
    42→- **Z** = vertical levels (up +)
    43→- Tile size: 32 px (`TILE_SIZE_PX`)
    44→- Player starts at (0, 0, z=1) on GRASS at z=0
    45→
    46→### World model
    47→
    48→```
    49→WorldMap
    50→  └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>           z-level → Layer
    51→     └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>      chunk pos → Chunk (32x32)
    52→        ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>        dense block storage
    53→        ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    sparse floor covers
    54→        └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  sparse ceiling covers
    55→```
    56→
    57→- **BlockVolume** wraps `BlockType` (u16), occupies z to z+1
    58→- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1
    59→- `block_at_feet` — block at player's z (usually AIR)
    60→- `block_below` — block under player (usually solid terrain)
    61→
    62→### Generation
    63→
    64→Noise-based procedural generation (FBM Perlin):
    65→
    66→| Noise | Octaves | Scale | Determines |
    67→|-------|---------|-------|------------|
    68→| Elevation | 6 | 0.008 | Terrain shape, z-level |
    69→| Moisture | 4 | 0.012 | Biome (grass/mud/sand) |
    70→| Temperature | 1 | 0.006 | Climate (snow/ice) |
    71→| Cave | 3 | 0.05 | Underground caverns |
    72→
    73→Elevation → surface z-level:
    74→
    75→| Elevation | Z | Terrain |
    76→|-----------|---|---------|
    77→| < ~0.12 | -2 | Deep water |
    78→| < 0.35 | -1 | Shallow water |
    79→| < 0.55 | 0 | Plains |
    80→| < 0.70 | 1 | Hills |
    81→| < 0.85 | 2 | Mountains |
    82→| >= 0.85 | 3 | Peaks |
    83→
    84→Water z-level uses a continuous formula: `z = -1 - ((0.35 - elevation) / 0.35 * 1.5) as i32`. Shore (0.35–0.40) generates SAND/ICE blocks at z=0.
    85→
    86→Generation depth: 5 below / 4 above active z-level. Stairs auto-placed at elevation transitions.
    87→
    88→### Surface index & grounding
    89→
    90→Surface index maps `(x, y)` columns to walkable z-levels. Grounding resolution determines movement:
    91→
    92→- Max step up: 1 block
    93→- Fall: unlimited (> 3 blocks = damage, not yet implemented)
    94→- Transitions: Walk, StepUp, StepDown, Fall, Blocked
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Block types
    99→
   100→| ID | Name | ASCII | blocks_movement | supports_standing | Notes |
   101→|----|------|:-----:|:---:|:---:|-------|
   102→| 0 | AIR | `.` | | | Empty space |
   103→| 1 | VOID | ` ` | x | | Impenetrable |
   104→| 2 | HOLE | `o` | | | Vertical shaft, see-through |
   105→| 10 | STONE | `#` | x | x | Solid rock |
   106→| 11 | DIRT | `#` | x | x | Underground fill |
   107→| 20 | GRASS | `#` | | x | Surface terrain |
   108→| 21 | SAND | `#` | | x | Beach / desert |
   109→| 22 | MUD | `#` | | x | Wet terrain |
   110→| 23 | DRY_GRASS | `#` | | x | Arid terrain |
   111→| 24 | SNOW | `#` | | x | Cold terrain |
   112→| 25 | ICE | `#` | x | x | Semi-transparent solid |
   113→| 40 | WALL_STONE | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
   114→| 41 | WALL_WOOD | `#` | x | | Constructed wall |
   115→| 42 | GLASS_WALL | `#` | x | | Transparent wall |
   116→| 50 | STAIRS_UP | `^` | | x | Ascending stairs |
   117→| 51 | STAIRS_DOWN | `v` | | x | Descending stairs |
   118→| 60 | DEEP_WATER | `~` | x | | Impassable water |
   119→| 61 | SHALLOW_WATER | `~` | | x | Wadeable |
   120→| 70 | LANTERN | `*` | | | Light source |
   121→
   122→Movement rule: target tile must not have `blocks_movement` at feet level AND must have `supports_standing` below.
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Controls
   127→
   128→### Mode switch
   129→
   130→| Key | Action |
   131→|-----|--------|
   132→| **B** | Toggle Adventure <-> Overseer mode |
   133→
   134→### Adventure mode
   135→
   136→#### Movement
   137→
   138→| Key | Action |
   139→|-----|--------|
   140→| **W/A/S/D** | Move North/West/South/East |
   141→| **Shift** | Sprint |
   142→| **< (Comma)** | Climb stairs up |
   143→| **> (Period)** | Descend stairs down |
   144→
   145→#### Block interaction
   146→
   147→| Input | Action |
   148→|-------|--------|
   149→| **LMB** | Mine block at cursor |
   150→| **RMB** | Place block from hotbar |
   151→| **Ctrl** | Modifier: floor level (z-1) |
   152→| **Alt** | Modifier: ceiling level (z+1) |
   153→| _(no modifier)_ | Wall level (z) |
   154→
   155→Cursor colors: white = wall, blue = floor, orange = ceiling, red = can mine, green = can place.
   156→
   157→#### Hotbar & inventory
   158→
   159→| Input | Action |
   160→|-------|--------|
   161→| **1-0** | Select hotbar slot 1-10 |
   162→| **Scroll wheel** | Cycle hotbar |
   163→| **Tab** | Toggle inventory |
   164→| **Esc** | Close inventory |
   165→| **Q** | Drop item (+Shift = stack, +Ctrl = half) |
   166→| **LMB outside slots** | Drop dragged stack (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |
   167→| **RMB outside slots** | Drop 1 dragged item (+Shift = 1, +Ctrl = half) |
   168→
   169→### Overseer mode
   170→
   171→#### Camera
   172→
   173→| Input | Action |
   174→|-------|--------|
   175→| **W/A/S/D** or **Arrows** | Pan camera |
   176→| **MMB drag** | Free pan |
   177→| **Scroll wheel** | Change z-level |
   178→| **PgUp / PgDn** | Jump z-level |
   179→
   180→#### Unit selection
   181→
   182→| Input | Action |
   183→|-------|--------|
   184→| **LMB click** | Select unit (empty = deselect) |
   185→| **LMB drag** | Box select |
   186→| **Shift+LMB** | Toggle / add to selection |
   187→
   188→#### Commands
   189→
   190→| Input | Action |
   191→|-------|--------|
   192→| **RMB** | Issue smart command |
   193→| **Shift+RMB** | Queue command (append) |
   194→| **Esc** | Reset tool to Auto mode |
   195→
   196→Smart action priority: Pickup item > Demolish block > Build (if hotbar has block) > MoveTo.
   197→
   198→Colors: green = move, blue = build, red = demolish, yellow = pickup.
   199→
   200→---
   201→
   202→## TCP debug protocol
   203→
   204→Game listens on `127.0.0.1:9999` (feature `debug-server`, default on). Protocol: JSON lines over TCP — one JSON object + `\n` per command.
   205→
   206→### Response format
   207→
   208→```json
   209→{"id": 1, "ok": true, "data": {...}}
   210→{"id": 2, "ok": false, "data": null, "error": "message"}
   211→```
   212→
   213→### Quick test
   214→
   215→```bash
   216→echo '{"cmd":"Ping"}' | nc -q1 127.0.0.1 9999
   217→```
   218→
   219→### Command reference
   220→
   221→<details>
   222→<summary>Meta</summary>
   223→
   224→| Command | JSON |
   225→|---------|------|
   226→| Ping | `{"cmd":"Ping"}` |
   227→| QueryInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryInfo"}` |
   228→| QueryGameInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryGameInfo"}` |
   229→
   230→</details>
   231→
   232→<details>
   233→<summary>Queries</summary>
   234→
   235→| Command | JSON |
   236→|---------|------|
   237→| QueryState | `{"cmd":"QueryState"}` |
   238→| QueryBlock | `{"cmd":"QueryBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` |
   239→| QuerySurfaces | `{"cmd":"QuerySurfaces","x":N,"y":N}` |
   240→| QueryCanMove | `{"cmd":"QueryCanMove","dir":"North"}` |
   241→| QueryBlocked | `{"cmd":"QueryBlocked"}` |
   242→| QueryLayer | `{"cmd":"QueryLayer","z":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N}` |
   243→| QueryCrossSection | `{"cmd":"QueryCrossSection","y":N,"x_min":N,"x_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` |
   244→| QueryVolume | `{"cmd":"QueryVolume","x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z_min":N,"z_max":N}` |
   245→| QueryNearby | `{"cmd":"QueryNearby","radius":N}` |
   246→| QueryFOV | `{"cmd":"QueryFOV","radius":N}` |
   247→
   248→</details>
   249→
   250→<details>
   251→<summary>Movement</summary>
   252→
   253→| Command | JSON |
   254→|---------|------|
   255→| MoveDir | `{"cmd":"MoveDir","dir":"North"}` |
   256→| MoveDelta | `{"cmd":"MoveDelta","dx":N,"dy":N}` |
   257→| StairsUp | `{"cmd":"StairsUp"}` |
   258→| StairsDown | `{"cmd":"StairsDown"}` |
   259→| Teleport | `{"cmd":"Teleport","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` |
   260→
   261→Directions: `"North"` (+Y), `"South"` (-Y), `"East"` (+X), `"West"` (-X).
   262→
   263→</details>
   264→
   265→<details>
   266→<summary>World modification</summary>
   267→
   268→| Command | JSON |
   269→|---------|------|
   270→| SetBlock | `{"cmd":"SetBlock","x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"block_type":"STONE"}` |
   271→| FillArea | `{"cmd":"FillArea","x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z":N,"block_type":"STONE"}` |
   272→| RebuildSurfaces | `{"cmd":"RebuildSurfaces"}` |
   273→
   274→</details>
   275→
   276→<details>
   277→<summary>Registry</summary>
   278→
   279→| Command | JSON |
   280→|---------|------|
   281→| QueryRegistry | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistry","kind":"blocks"}` |
   282→| QueryRegistryLookup | `{"cmd":"QueryRegistryLookup","kind":"items","query":"apple"}` |
   283→
   284→Kinds: `"blocks"`, `"items"`, `"floor_covers"`.
   285→
   286→</details>
   287→
   288→<details>
   289→<summary>Unit management</summary>
   290→
   291→| Command | JSON |
   292→|---------|------|
   293→| QueryUnits | `{"cmd":"QueryUnits"}` |
   294→| QueryUnitInfo | `{"cmd":"QueryUnitInfo","entity_id":N}` |
   295→| UnitMoveTo | `{"cmd":"UnitMoveTo","entity_id":N,"x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` |
   296→| UnitBuild | `{"cmd":"UnitBuild","entity_id":N,"x":N,"y":N,"z":N,"item_id":N}` |
   297→| UnitDemolish | `{"cmd":"UnitDemolish","entity_id":N,"x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` |
   298→| UnitPickup | `{"cmd":"UnitPickup","entity_id":N,"target_entity_id":N}` |
   299→| UnitQueueClear | `{"cmd":"UnitQueueClear","entity_id":N}` |
   300→| UnitStop | `{"cmd":"UnitStop","entity_id":N}` |
   301→
   302→</details>
   303→
   304→<details>
   305→<summary>Task Management</summary>
   306→
   307→| Command | JSON |
   308→|---------|------|
   309→| QueryTasks | `{"cmd":"QueryTasks","status":"all"}` |
   310→| TaskCreate | `{"cmd":"TaskCreate","kind":"demolish","x":N,"y":N,"z":N}` |
   311→| TaskCancel | `{"cmd":"TaskCancel","task_id":N}` |
   312→| TaskCancelZone | `{"cmd":"TaskCancelZone","x_min":N,"x_max":N,"y_min":N,"y_max":N,"z":N}` |
   313→
   314→</details>
   315→
   316→---
   317→
   318→## kinswardctl — CLI for game control
   319→
   320→`kinswardctl` is a command-line tool for controlling the running game via TCP debug server. A wrapper over the protocol above with human-readable output.
   321→
   322→### Setup
   323→
   324→```bash
   325→# Game must be running
   326→cargo run
   327→
   328→# In another terminal:
   329→cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- ping
   330→```
   331→
   332→### Global flags
   333→
   334→| Flag | Description |
   335→|------|-------------|
   336→| `--host <IP>` | Server address (default `127.0.0.1`) |
   337→| `--port <PORT>` | Port (default `9999`) |
   338→| `--json` | Raw JSON output |
   339→
   340→```bash
   341→kinswardctl --json state          # raw JSON
   342→kinswardctl --port 8888 ping      # custom port
   343→```
   344→
   345→### Meta
   346→
   347→```bash
   348→kinswardctl ping                  # test connection
   349→kinswardctl info                  # active_z and basic info
   350→kinswardctl game-info             # seed, layers, chunks, entities
   351→```
   352→
   353→### Player state
   354→
   355→```bash
   356→kinswardctl state
   357→# OK  x=0  y=0  z=1  active_z=1  block_at_feet=AIR  block_below=GRASS  has_support=true
   358→
   359→kinswardctl blocked
   360→# OK  blocked: West, North
   361→
   362→kinswardctl can-move north
   363→# OK  North can_move=false
   364→```
   365→
   366→### Movement
   367→
   368→```bash
   369→kinswardctl move north            # one tile (n/s/e/w)
   370→kinswardctl move-delta 3 0        # offset dx=3, dy=0
   371→kinswardctl stairs up             # climb stairs
   372→kinswardctl stairs down           # descend stairs
   373→kinswardctl teleport 10 5 2       # teleport to (10, 5, z=2)
   374→```
   375→
   376→### World visualization
   377→
   378→```bash
   379→# Top-down layer at z=1, x=-5..5, y=-5..5
   380→kinswardctl layer -z 1 -x -5:5 -y -5:5
   381→
   382→# Side cross-section at y=0, x=-5..5, z=0..3
   383→kinswardctl cross-section -y 0 -x -5:5 -z 0:3
   384→
   385→# 3D volume data (JSON)
   386→kinswardctl volume -x -2:2 -y -2:2 -z 0:2
   387→
   388→# Field of view (ASCII visibility map)
   389→kinswardctl fov --radius 12
   390→
   391→# Nearby items
   392→kinswardctl nearby --radius 10
   393→# OK  3 items within radius 10 of (0,0,1)
   394→#   [4294967302] (1,2) Stone x5  dist=2
   395→#   [4294967310] (-1,0) Apple x1  dist=1
   396→```
   397→
   398→### World modification
   399→
   400→```bash
   401→kinswardctl set-block 5 5 1 STONE
   402→# OK  set (5,5,1) to STONE
   403→
   404→kinswardctl fill -x -3:3 -y -3:3 -z 1 AIR
   405→# OK  filled 49 blocks with AIR
   406→
   407→kinswardctl rebuild-surfaces       # usually auto, manual if needed
   408→```
   409→
   410→### Registry
   411→
   412→```bash
   413→kinswardctl registry blocks        # all block types
   414→kinswardctl registry items         # all items
   415→kinswardctl registry floor-covers  # floor covers
   416→
   417→kinswardctl lookup blocks STONE    # lookup by name or id
   418→kinswardctl lookup items apple     # search items (substring, case-insensitive)
   419→kinswardctl lookup items 102       # search by numeric id
   420→```
   421→
   422→### Unit management
   423→
   424→Units execute queued commands autonomously with A* pathfinding. Works in any input mode.
   425→
   426→```bash
   427→# List units (get entity IDs)
   428→kinswardctl units
   429→# OK  2 unit(s)
   430→#   ID                   Name             Position     Queue  Status
   431→#   4294967305           Worker           (3,2,1)      0      idle
   432→
   433→# Detailed info
   434→kinswardctl unit-info 4294967305
   435→# OK  Unit: Worker (id=4294967305)
   436→#   Position: (3,2,1)
   437→#   Status: idle  Hotbar slot: 0
   438→#   Inventory (2 items):
   439→#     [0] Stone Block x10
   440→#     [1] Apple x3
   441→#   Queue: (empty)
   442→
   443→# Move unit to target
   444→kinswardctl unit-move 4294967305 10 5 1
   445→# OK  queued (queue_len=1)
   446→
   447→# Build block (item_id from registry items)
   448→kinswardctl unit-build 4294967305 10 5 1 102
   449→# OK  queued (queue_len=2)
   450→
   451→# Demolish block
   452→kinswardctl unit-demolish 4294967305 10 6 1
   453→
   454→# Pick up item (target_entity_id from nearby)
   455→kinswardctl unit-pickup 4294967305 4294967310
   456→
   457→# Multi-waypoint route (single TCP connection)
   458→kinswardctl unit-plan 4294967305 5,0,1 5,5,1 0,5,1
   459→#   1. 5,0,1 -> queued (queue_len=1)
   460→#   2. 5,5,1 -> queued (queue_len=2)
   461→#   3. 0,5,1 -> queued (queue_len=3)
   462→# OK  3 waypoints queued for unit 4294967305
   463→
   464→# Clear queue (unit finishes current path segment)
   465→kinswardctl unit-queue-clear 4294967305
   466→
   467→# Full stop (immediate)
   468→kinswardctl unit-stop 4294967305
   469→```
   470→
   471→### Task management
   472→
   473→Global task queue decouples task creation from unit assignment. Tasks are auto-assigned to idle units.
   474→
   475→```bash
   476→kinswardctl tasks                                  # list all tasks
   477→kinswardctl tasks --status pending                 # filter: pending/assigned/blocked
   478→kinswardctl task-add demolish 5 5 1                # create task
   479→kinswardctl task-add build 5 5 1 --item 102        # create build task
   480→kinswardctl task-cancel 42                         # cancel task by ID
   481→kinswardctl task-cancel-zone -x 0:5 -y 0:5 -z 1   # cancel zone tasks
   482→```
   483→
   484→### Raw JSON
   485→
   486→```bash
   487→kinswardctl raw '{"cmd":"QueryState"}'
   488→```
   489→
   490→### Help
   491→
   492→```bash
   493→kinswardctl --help                 # all commands
   494→kinswardctl move --help            # help for specific command
   495→```
   496→
   497→### Tips
   498→
   499→- Start with `state` to get player position and surroundings
   500→- `blocked` is more efficient than 4 `can-move` calls
   501→- `layer` and `cross-section` for quick spatial orientation
   502→- `fill` instead of many `set-block` for bulk operations
   503→- `unit-plan` sends multiple waypoints over a single TCP connection
   504→- `unit-stop` for immediate halt; `unit-queue-clear` lets unit finish current path
   505→- `nearby` returns `entity_id` for items — use with `unit-pickup`
   506→- `--json` flag goes before subcommand: `kinswardctl --json state`
   507→

1→name: Release Build
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    tags:
     6→      - "v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+*"
     7→
     8→env:
     9→  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
    10→  BINARY_NAME: kinsward
    11→  VERSION: ${{ github.ref_name }}
    12→
    13→permissions:
    14→  contents: write
    15→
    16→jobs:
    17→  build:
    18→    strategy:
    19→      matrix:
    20→        include:
    21→          - os: ubuntu-latest
    22→            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
    23→            suffix: linux-x86_64
    24→            binary_ext: ""
    25→          - os: windows-latest
    26→            target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
    27→            suffix: windows-x86_64
    28→            binary_ext: ".exe"
    29→
    30→    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    31→
    32→    steps:
    33→      - name: Checkout
    34→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    35→
    36→      - name: Install Rust toolchain
    37→        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
    38→
    39→      - name: Cache cargo registry & build
    40→        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
    41→        with:
    42→          key: ${{ matrix.target }}
    43→
    44→      # Linux: install Bevy system dependencies
    45→      - name: Install Linux dependencies
    46→        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
    47→        run: |
    48→          sudo apt-get update
    49→          sudo apt-get install -y \
    50→            pkg-config \
    51→            libx11-dev \
    52→            libasound2-dev \
    53→            libudev-dev \
    54→            libxkbcommon-x11-dev \
    55→            libwayland-dev \
    56→            libxkbcommon-dev \
    57→            libvulkan-dev
    58→
    59→      - name: Build release
    60→        run: cargo build --release --no-default-features
    61→
    62→      - name: Prepare artifact (Linux)
    63→        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
    64→        run: |
    65→          mkdir -p artifact
    66→          cp target/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }} artifact/
    67→          cp -r assets artifact/ 2>/dev/null || true
    68→          cd artifact
    69→          tar czf ../${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.tar.gz *
    70→
    71→      - name: Prepare artifact (Windows)
    72→        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
    73→        shell: pwsh
    74→        run: |
    75→          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path artifact
    76→          Copy-Item "target/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}.exe" artifact/
    77→          if (Test-Path "assets") { Copy-Item -Recurse "assets" artifact/ }
    78→          Compress-Archive -Path artifact/* -DestinationPath "${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.zip"
    79→
    80→      - name: Upload artifact
    81→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
    82→        with:
    83→          name: ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}
    84→          path: |
    85→            ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.tar.gz
    86→            ${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${{ env.VERSION }}-${{ matrix.suffix }}.zip
    87→          if-no-files-found: ignore
    88→
    89→  release:
    90→    needs: build
    91→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    92→
    93→    steps:
    94→      - name: Download all artifacts
    95→        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
    96→        with:
    97→          path: artifacts
    98→          merge-multiple: true
    99→
   100→      - name: Create GitHub Release
   101→        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
   102→        with:
   103→          generate_release_notes: true
   104→          files: |
   105→            artifacts/*.tar.gz
   106→            artifacts/*.zip
   107→

1→//! Global system ordering sets.
     2→//!
     3→//! Each set represents a logical phase of the game loop.
     4→//! Replaces fragile `.after(concrete_function)` ordering with centralized sets.
     5→
     6→use bevy::prelude::*;
     7→
     8→use crate::game_phase::GamePhase;
     9→
    10→#[derive(SystemSet, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
    11→pub enum GameSet {
    12→    /// Player input processing (WASD, mouse, keyboard)
    13→    Input,
    14→    /// Command processing (TCP, overseer commands)
    15→    Commands,
    16→    /// Player/unit movement, stair transitions, terrain sync
    17→    Movement,
    18→    /// Field of view computation
    19→    Fov,
    20→    /// Lighting computation (after FOV)
    21→    Lighting,
    22→    /// Elevation barrier updates (after FOV + movement)
    23→    Barriers,
    24→    /// Tile rendering updates (after lighting + FOV)
    25→    Render,
    26→}
    27→
    28→pub struct SchedulePlugin;
    29→
    30→impl Plugin for SchedulePlugin {
    31→    fn build(&self, app: &mut App) {
    32→        let playing = in_state(GamePhase::Playing);
    33→        app.configure_sets(
    34→            Update,
    35→            (
    36→                GameSet::Input.run_if(playing.clone()),
    37→                GameSet::Commands.after(GameSet::Input).run_if(playing.clone()),
    38→                GameSet::Movement
    39→                    .after(GameSet::Input)
    40→                    .after(GameSet::Commands)
    41→                    .run_if(playing.clone()),
    42→                GameSet::Fov.after(GameSet::Movement).run_if(playing.clone()),
    43→                GameSet::Lighting.after(GameSet::Fov).run_if(playing.clone()),
    44→                GameSet::Barriers.after(GameSet::Fov).run_if(playing.clone()),
    45→                GameSet::Render
    46→                    .after(GameSet::Lighting)
    47→                    .after(GameSet::Barriers)
    48→                    .run_if(playing),
    49→            ),
    50→        );
    51→    }
    52→}
    53→

1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Build & Run
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→cargo run                              # default (debug features: TCP server + inspector)
     9→cargo run --features dev               # dynamic linking for faster recompilation
    10→cargo build --no-default-features      # release-like build without debug tools
    11→cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- <cmd>        # CLI debug tool (game must be running)
    12→cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- region-map elevation   # export region map layer as PNG
    13→cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42                              # headless worldgen, all layers as PNG
    14→cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --layers elevation --size 1024
    15→cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --map-size 128 --format stats --json  # fast stats
    16→```
    17→
    18→**Feature flags**: `debug` (default) = `debug-server` + `bevy-inspector-egui`; `dev` = `debug` + dynamic linking.
    19→
    20→**Rust toolchain**: nightly required.
    21→
    22→## Testing
    23→
    24→```bash
    25→cargo test                             # all tests
    26→cargo test <test_name>                 # single test
    27→cargo test --lib world::chunk          # tests in a specific module
    28→```
    29→
    30→Tests are inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` within source files, primarily in `src/world/` modules.
    31→
    32→## Architecture
    33→
    34→**Engine**: Bevy 0.18 ECS + avian2d physics. Every subsystem is a Bevy Plugin registered in `main.rs`.
    35→
    36→### System execution order (GameSet)
    37→
    38→```
    39→Input -> Commands -> Movement -> Fov -> Lighting -> Barriers -> Render
    40→```
    41→
    42→Defined in `src/schedule.rs`. Systems must be added to the correct set or ordering breaks.
    43→
    44→### World model
    45→
    46→```
    47→WorldMap (Resource, src/world/map.rs)
    48→  └─ HashMap<i32, Layer>           z-level → Layer (src/world/layer.rs)
    49→     └─ HashMap<IVec2, Chunk>      chunk_pos → Chunk 32×32 (src/world/chunk.rs)
    50→        ├─ Vec<BlockVolume>        dense block grid
    51→        ├─ HashMap<(u8,u8), FloorCoverId>    sparse floor covers
    52→        └─ HashMap<(u8,u8), CeilingCoverId>  sparse ceiling covers
    53→```
    54→
    55→- `CHUNK_SIZE = 32`, `TILE_SIZE_PX = 32.0`
    56→- `BlockType` is a u16 newtype (not enum) with named constants — extensible without variant explosion
    57→- `BlockRegistry` stores `BlockProps` in a flat `Vec<Option<BlockProps>>` indexed by u16 for O(1) lookup
    58→- Block definitions loaded from `assets/blocks.ron` (RON with `IMPLICIT_SOME` extension), hardcoded fallback if missing
    59→- Item definitions from `assets/items.ron` — both loaded via `std::fs::read_to_string`, not Bevy AssetServer
    60→
    61→### Climate pipeline (`src/generation/climate/`)
    62→
    63→RegionMap 1024×1024 grid, each cell = 16×16 tiles. Pipeline in `src/generation/mod.rs::init_region_map`:
    64→
    65→```
    66→ 1. Plates + Elevation     Voronoi plates, boundary uplift, detail noise
    67→ 2. Terrain Conditioning   thermal erosion, smoothing
    68→ 3. Sea Level              ocean/coast/land BFS, ocean_distance
    69→ 4. Temperature            latitude + lapse rate + ocean proximity
    70→ 5. Pressure               Hadley + thermal + barometric + noise + pressure centers
    71→ 6. Wind                   pressure gradient + Coriolis + terrain deflection + drag
    72→ 7. Rainfall               evaporation + advection + orographic + rain shadow
    73→ 8. Geology                rock types from tectonic context (8 types: RockType enum)
    74→ 9. Depression Fill        priority-flood to remove micro-sinks
    75→10. Drainage               elevation + slope + rock permeability
    76→11. Proto-Rivers           flow accumulation + lake filling
    77→12. Fluvial Erosion        river valleys, modulated by rock hardness
    78→13. Final Rivers           recompute on eroded terrain
    79→14. Biomes                 temperature × rainfall × elevation × drainage (22 BiomeId variants)
    80→15. Fertility              soil fertility from rain + temp + drainage + elevation + rivers
    81→16. Vegetation             plant density from biome + rainfall + temperature + noise
    82→```
    83→
    84→Map view layers: Elevation, Biome, Temperature, Rainfall, Wind, Drainage, Rivers, Geology, Fertility, Vegetation, Plates.
    85→Export: `cargo run --bin kinswardctl -- region-map <layer>` (PNG/ASCII/stats).
    86→
    87→### Coordinate system
    88→
    89→- X = East(+)/West(-), Y = North(+)/South(-), Z = vertical levels (up +)
    90→- Player at z=N stands on block at z=N-1
    91→- World coords: `tile_x = (translation.x / TILE_SIZE_PX).round() as i32`
    92→
    93→### Key patterns
    94→
    95→**Dirty flags** (`DirtyFlags` resource in `src/world/mod.rs`): tracks what needs recomputation (`active_z_changed`, `render_dirty`, `lighting_dirty`, `fov_dirty`, etc.). Systems check flags and early-return if clean. `DirtyTiles` in render provides per-tile granularity.
    96→
    97→**Command system** (`src/commands/`): All game actions route through `GameCommand` enum (serde-tagged `#[serde(tag = "cmd")]`). Commands pushed to `CommandQueue` resource, processed each frame by `process_commands` / `process_unit_commands`. TCP debug server (port 9999) accepts JSON lines, routes to CommandQueue. Unit commands are partitioned from global commands to avoid ECS query conflicts.
    98→
    99→**Task system** (`src/overseer/task_queue.rs`): Global `TaskQueue` resource stores abstract tasks (Build/Demolish/Pickup) with statuses (Pending/Assigned/Blocked). `TaskAssigner` system auto-assigns Pending tasks to idle units. Failed tasks return to queue as Blocked with retry cooldown. Tasks are created by zone commands (UI) and `TaskCreate` (TCP). TCP commands: QueryTasks, TaskCreate, TaskCancel, TaskCancelZone.
   100→
   101→**State machine** (`src/overseer/mode.rs`): `InputMode` state (`Adventure` | `Overseer`), toggled with B key. Systems use `.run_if(in_state(...))` guards. `OverseerToolMode` is a sub-state for tool selection.
   102→
   103→**UnitZ component**: Tracks z-level independently from `Transform` (which only has 2D x,y). Both Player and Units use this.
   104→
   105→**Surface index** (`src/world/surface_index.rs`): Maps (x,y) columns to walkable z-levels. `resolve_grounding()` in `grounding.rs` determines movement transitions (Walk, StepUp, StepDown, Fall, Blocked).
   106→
   107→### Rendering
   108→
   109→- Tile-based: one Bevy entity per visible tile, managed by `src/render/viewport.rs`
   110→- Sprites from 10px-cell texture atlases (Natural.png, Civilized.png, People.png) scaled 3.2× to match TILE_SIZE_PX
   111→- Final tile color = base_color × depth_factor × light_value × visibility
   112→- Overlay blocks (e.g. LANTERN) render as separate entity on top of base tile
   113→- `SpriteAssets` loaded in `PreStartup` via `TextureAtlasLayout::from_grid()`
   114→
   115→### Module layout
   116→
   117→| Module | Purpose |
   118→|--------|---------|
   119→| `src/world/` | WorldMap, blocks, chunks, layers, surfaces, grounding |
   120→| `src/commands/` | GameCommand enum, executor, TCP debug server, region-map export |
   121→| `src/render/` | Viewport rendering, sprite assets, FPS display |
   122→| `src/fov/` | Shadowcasting FOV, visibility maps |
   123→| `src/lighting/` | Light propagation, ambient/emitter lights |
   124→| `src/generation/` | Procedural world generation, climate pipeline (16 steps) |
   125→| `src/generation/climate/` | Plates, elevation, temperature, pressure, wind, rainfall, geology, drainage, rivers, biomes, fertility, vegetation |
   126→| `src/units/` | Unit components, spawner, possession |
   127→| `src/overseer/` | RTS mode: camera, selection, commands, pathfinding, task queue, UI |
   128→| `src/companions/` | Companion behavior (follow, station, autonomous) |
   129→| `src/ui/` | UI animation system |
   130→
   131→Flat modules: `player.rs` (player entity/movement), `movement_rules.rs` (traversal logic), `physics.rs` (collision layers), `schedule.rs` (system ordering), `items.rs`/`inventory.rs`/`hotbar.rs` (item system), `block_interaction.rs`/`block_item.rs` (block interactions), `camera.rs` (camera control), `elevation_barrier.rs` (elevation barriers), `input.rs` (input handling), `pickup_drop.rs` (pickup/drop), `proximity.rs` (proximity detection), `tooltip.rs` (tooltips), `world_item.rs` (world items).
   132→
   133→## Conventions
   134→
   135→- **Plugins**: `<Name>Plugin`, registered in `main.rs`
   136→- **Components**: PascalCase (`Player`, `UnitZ`, `TileRender`, `PathFollower`)
   137→- **Systems**: snake_case verbs (`spawn_player`, `compute_fov`, `process_commands`)
   138→- **Events**: PascalCase + "Event" suffix (`BlockChangedEvent`, `SpawnUnitEvent`)
   139→- **Feature gates**: `#[cfg(feature = "...")]` inline, not separate files
   140→- **Movement rules**: target tile must NOT have `blocks_movement` at feet level AND must have `supports_standing` below
   141→- All terrain blocks are SOLID (Minecraft-like) — "floor" is a decorative surface cover, not a block type
   142→- Only non-blocking blocks: STAIRS_UP, STAIRS_DOWN, LANTERN, SHALLOW_WATER
   143→- 51 unused warnings exist — known tech debt, don't fix unless touching that code
   144→
